content: The Squad looks at the schema of the Computer Core level, C1, with interest as they decide and plot their next move.

Lexi leaves her team to formulate a plan while she focuses on recovering from her latest excursion to recover the white datacard. Once her wounds are taken care of she takes fifteen minutes to center herself in the Force.
Standing from her meditation so approaches Bishop and Magnet. "**Is there any update to the status of the other teams?**"

**"No signficant updates. As far as we can tell by monitoring the Comm bands, using our decryption, the Nighthawks are still engaged in a battle on the Detention Level. There are also reports of, what we assume to be, Captain Thrax's Commando units stirring up trouble on multiple other levels as well."**
**"Most of the base's attention and resources seem to be directed at the Water Pump Station level, however."**
**"Some bad news there, one of the holocams must have caught us on the retreat, and now there is a Wanted Holoposter on the base Holonet, with your likeness Commander."**
**"It doesn't do your beauty justice, Commander. Truly."**
**"What did we say about hitting on the Commander, Grave..."**
Lexi frowns and gives a small shrug. "**It was only a matter of time before they picked up I'm not really a Reaper...**" she says thoughtfully.
**"Simply relaying operational details, XO, that's all."**
**"That Computer Access could really come in handy right about now, Commander..."**
"**No chatter regarding our movements here?**"
**"Not yet, nothing to report as yet I imagine."**
"**One blessing.**" Lexi looks over at NZ. "**We have the white keycard, but Dr Banos needs to show NZ how to access the systems with it. He and I will go to his lab.**"

**"Finally some good news... the next order of business would be to set up that Computer Access Terminal for Magnet. Then we leave Magnet here on Level C1, so he can coordinate our further infiltration by opening up any needed doors or shutting off holocams along our next route. It would also be smart to leave at least one Lookout alongside Magnet to ware for enemy movements, while Magnet is distracted by the Computer Terminal someone should be guarding him."**
**"I would be delighted to take the role, Master, and, if needed, I could sneak out to rejoin you at any time, thanks to my unmatched stealth configuration."**
**"Don't forget, though, in order to get deeper access to the base computer protocols, I would still request of you to liberate a code cylinder from a higher ranking base officer."**
**"The Droid seems to know quite a bit about Tech, maybe we should leave him on this level alone, and that way I could still monitor Communications if I remain with the squad."**

**"Might be dangerous to leave a Droid alone and unsupervised, they are intelligent when it comes to interacting with computers, but when it comes to dealing with Stormtroopers or Reapers... Perhaps not so much."**

"**A droid is less likely to draw attention to himself than any of you.**" Lexi remarks as she listens to the suggestions. "**NZ  is better able to extricate himself covertly should he draw unwanted attention.**" She nods as the decision is made in her mind. "**Once we get the access NZ. We'll find you somewhere safe to hole up. Magnet will stay with the squad.**"
**"Aknowledged, Commander."**
**"Certainly Master, I am supremely not-excited, Master."**
"**It will only be temporary, NZ. We'll have you rejoin us as soon as we secure the next obstacle. **"
After completely healing up the members of the Squad, Lexi and NZ make ready to revisit Doctor Banos.
Arriving near the entry to Laboratory 2, the doors open automatically.
Lexi steps in cautiously, looking around. "**Expecting us Doctor?**" she asks once she sees the laboratory is all clear.
**"My door is always open... I believe that's how we met to begin with."**
**"Look at what you've done to my droid... Well, its a small price to pay if Doctors Xass and Xish have indeed been ... retired."**
Lexi motions to NZ for him to show Banos the white keycard. "**They've been dealt with,**" she says leaving out that they were just sedated and not actually dead.

**"Good, good..."** He seems to nod to himself in satisfaction. **"You've truly outlived my expectations. Of course... You're simply a Hand of Thrawn, so your excellence is merely a reflection of that singular Chiss mind... do not forget that."***

Lexi decides to ignore the rub, she wasn't so prideful to let it interfere with the task at hand. It didn't matter to her who got credit as long as she was able to complete her task. "**Will you be able to repair NZ? He sustained a great deal of damage during the fight with Xish.**"
**"Of course I can repair him, I *built* him didn't I? Now I'm wishing I hadn't sold it to Senior Captain Thrawn... but at that particular point in life, I needed the credits."**

Banos motions to a workstation, where cables plug into NZ and he is placed on a worktable, where automatic drills and fusion torches begin disassembly, parts replacement, and reassembly all at an incredibly fast pace.

**"Repairs complete. Since the time when I had built him, I've discovered many more advancements in Droid research. I've taken the liberty of upgrading NZ with all of the state of the art components. He should be more effective in just about every regard, from now on. Yes yes, I know, I'm a genius."**
**"Master, I can confirm that all my systems are running at 157% performance fraction of nominal."**

Lexi glances over NZ, nodding appreciatively. "**Thank you, Doctor. You have been quite generous.**" She turns back to the Chiss. "**You also offered to show NZ how to access the systems using the keycard.**"

**"Yes, he's been programmed with new instructions. You'll need to travel to the Central Turbolift and obtain a Remote Access Terminal, bring it with you back to this level once you're ready to link in. Just don't use my laboratory when you do that part, I don't want suspicion cast upon my workspace."**
**"And remember, I'm not doing this from generosity, I expect you to destroy the Queen, if you wish my continued patronage..."**

"**Yes.. I know, but to get to her, we need the terminal. Unless you have an idea of how we can get to her without it...**" She leaves it open for him to offer his suggestion before continuing. "**Accessing the Central Turbolift is going to be problematic with that AT-ST guarding it.**"

**"Unfortunately, my strengths lie in other fields of study. For infiltration, I invented a Droid that would do any and such work in my stead."** He pointedly tilts his head at NZ, who is standing with his back straight and swivels his neck back and forth, looking between the two of you.
**"Master, I believe we can either sneak by, or destroy the AT-ST, the latter option could be quite noisy, however."**
**"I recommend trying the first approach, and falling back on the latter approach if we're detected."**
His wrist give a lavish flourish. **"There you are."**
"**We... as in you and me? I'm not exactly the most stealthy, either.**"
**"I believe there is a trooper named Ghost, who could be ordered to perform this task, Master."**
**"However... He may not be as good when it comes to fighting an AT-ST as you are... Master."**
"**He's been incommunicado since we arrived. Otherwise I would fully agree with you.**"
**"Although my ceilings have been raised, this is still the new limit to my droid calculatory abilities, Master."**
**"Well, we can't make them too smart, otherwise they'll just replace us right?"** He shrugs.
**"There is one other option... If you could just convince the guards that you belong down at the Computer Core, they would be forced to stand down the AT-ST."**
**"However, if they detect anything to be wrong when you descend that Turboshaft... You should expect a very 'warm' reception once you return back to this level, if you catch my meaning."**
"**There's a problem with that plan. I've been placed on a Wanted list. There's a high chance they might recognize me.**"
"*Unless you have the means for me to affect a disguise.**"

**"In that case, you should probably effect a disguise... or disable the AT-ST in some other way that doesn't alert the rest of this level to your presence. Besides, its not as though every Trooper knows every new Wantee by heart, especially if you've only just been detected... and from the latest news I'm reading here, it appears that everyone's attention is on the guerilla fight going on at the edge of the jungle against the landed forces of... Thrawn I assume?"**
**"As I recall... Doctor Gylvane's body type is similar to yours..."**
"**Doctor Gylvane?**" she asks raising an eyebrow in question.
"**Not one of the researchers you sent me after before, I presume?**"
**"Heavens no, she's the vision of beauty that occupies Laboratory 6."**
**"Perhaps you can convince her to lend you some of her ... more official garments."**
"**She's no longer in her lab, maybe she's left something. Can we use the keycard to get access?**"
**"Only to Laboratory 7 with that card Im afraid."**
**"How do you know she's no longer in her laboratory?"**
"**Because we confronted her before heading to lab 7.**"
**"Well then, if not in her laboratory where is she?"**
"**She was on her way out to somewhere in the southern side of the level.**"

**"Might I suggest searching for her next, while she's still outside of the protection of her laboratory. You can either steal her Keycard and enter her laboratory to effect a new disguise, or convince her to aid you of her own volition. Now please... I have important research to conduct... I have no desire to spend all evening discussing business. I believe I've been more than just passively helpful to you... If you want more of my patronage, I expect you to at least do something about my lack of life partner at the moment... If you put in a good word with her, I would be especially generous, should your aid materialize in a most wonderous way."**
"**Of course, Doctor.. Thank you for your assistance,**" she glances at NZ and gives him a nod. They had overstayed their welcome in the laboratory.  "**Let's return to the others.**"
**"Commander."** He appears behind Lexi and N-Zee as they are walking the halls to return to the Med Bay.

Lexi turns to look back at the trooper suddenly appearing behind them. Cautiously she looks around to make sure they are indeed alone in the corridor. "**Ghost,**" she says quietly. "**You've found something?**"
**"About the Reaper Scientist, I can try to track her down, if you want."**
"**You've been following us?**" she asks not surprised that Ghost had been moving around relatively undected. "**Have you scouted the southern part of the level at all?**"
**"A bit yeah, to both questions."**
"**Alright... Find her for me. If you can find out more about her in the process I can use that would be even better.**"
**"As ordered, Commander."** He disappears again.
**"Welcome back Commander."**

Lexi steps into the medbay where the squad has been keeping low. "**Thank you, Commander. We saw Ghost on the way back. I have him doing some recon for me. Until he returns, we should start making plans to get past the AT-ST into the central turbolift.**" she says coming further into the room to look at the map they'd been slowly filling out. "**Ghost is looking for a Reaper who I may be able to impersonate and use her identity to more easily get past the AT-ST without us having to raise any alarms, but we should come up with an alternative plan.**"

**"The data my Maker implanted in my memory core will be of high interest, I believe, Master. It suggests that in order to access the Center Core of the Base Computer, someone must descend and check out a Remote Transceiver, that can access the Core from any location within the base wirelessly. This Transceiver is typically utilized to help in research projects, but because it holds access to the Core it can also be used to essentially control the whole Cyber Security apparatus."**

**"Theoretically all that's necessary is to use the White Keycard, but the problem is that, the staff guarding these Transceivers would of course cross reference the Keycard and the person presenting it."**
**"Of course to render Cyber Security access would also take a Code Cylinder. High level officers and researchers are the ones to check these Transceivers out from circulation."**
**"They resemble suitcase-computers."**
"**What sort of cross references? Would disguising myself and using Doctor Glyvane keycard be sufficient? Or will we need to find a way to get her to cooperate with us?**"

**"The identity of the person using the Keycard is cross referenced manually by Stormtroopers, but they don't exactly know everyone in this massive base on a first name basis. They would do the standard security check of: size, hair color, weight, etc."**
**"If they were really, really paying attention. Otherwise they might just waive you through, given that you have the right Keycard."**
"**There is one problem then... we've seen her and she's a Zabrak. We'd have to hope there's no mention of that detail.**"
**"No doubt its documented, its just a matter of how hard the Stormtroopers look, I suppose."**
**"I'm also unsure of the species of the clawed lizard Sith female."**
**"Probably part human, part something else..."**
"**The something else didn't become prevelant until she took that serum. It's possible I could pass as her in a pinch, and we already have her subdued.**"
"**Are you able to access any of her records with the keycard, NZ? Anything that may help if I were to use her identity? **"
**"Unfortunately the lab was destroyed during the battle, Master, leaving no records I can easily access."**

"**Shame. We shall see what Ghost can turn up then. In the meantime, perhaps we can create a distraction which would preoccupy the stormtroopers enough to not take too close attention to my credentials,**" she says thoughtfully.
**"I'm back, Commander. The female Zabrak scientist has returned to her Laboratory."**
**"She seemed in good spirits, when I last spotted her."**
"**Any indication as to where she came from?**"
**"Somewhere South, as you suspected, Commander."**
Lexi nods. "**Well... time to pay the good doctor a visit. NZ, Veq. You're with me.**"
**"Yes, Master."**
**"I shall assist your wishes, Chosen One."**

Once again, the trio find them selves nervously trotting down narrow featureless corridors and awkwardly trying to avoid walking directly into imperials, all the while attempting to blend in. Luckily, no one runs into them face to face, and they are smart enough to wait out the AT-ST's footsteps to be far away, before once again, approaching and hitting the buzzer at Laboratory 6.
A droid eye-stalk opens. A gentle female voice intones. **"You again?"**
Veq touches his temple sending out empathic waves outwardly.
"**Apologies for disturbing you, Doctor, but there's a matter we need to discuss with you.**"
**"You're lucky I still haven't undressed and showered for the evening... Come in, you have one minute to state your business."**

Laboratory 6 is a neat and bright place, strange electronic devices of unfamiliar design are in various stages of construction, the whole place resembling a disorganized warehouse of computer guts, electrical globes, and oddly shaped electrical conduits filling every square meter near to full. After a short second, Doctor Gylvane's heels can be heard clicking and she approaches you directly, putting one hand on her hip and striking a pose in her long heels and skimpy outfit.
The air around his head pulses in Lexi's Verdant Sight and Gylvane's pose softens slightly.

Lexi steps in, taking note of the various electronics neatly organized in the laboratory.  "**Thank you for seeing us, Doctor,**"she says, delaying long enough for the door to close behind them. "**We're actually here as a favor to a fellow researcher: Doctor Banos.**" Remembering the reaction of Xass and Xish when she had mentioned she'd been sent she holds up her hands placatingly. "**He sends his admiration and hopes you might be open to a visit from him to discuss your mutual interests,**" she motions towards the various technical components nearby.
**"Really?"** She blushes, **"Doctor Banos wants to discuss mutual interests?"**

**"Uh... sorry for the mess! Would you mind please... uh, telling Doctor Banos that I am very very busy, but I might be able to squeeze him into my tight schedule... So, uh, wait did he really send you?"**
Lexi gives the woman a smile. "**Oh yes. He called you a vision of beauty.**"
She covers her mouth, turning around as she blushes even deeper.
"**You know.. his door is always open,**" she suggests.
**"Oh-Okay -- I'll keep that in, in mind."** She coughs into a fist and turns around straightening out her dress. **"I haven't seen you Reapers here before, are you Projects of Doctor Banos?"**
"**We just recently began assisting him.**"
The Doctor comes up to Lexi and enters her personal space, squeezing a cheek as if checking the turgor of the skin.
**"Such high quality work."**
**"My compliments to your designer."**
Her scent is intoxicating and sweet.

Lexi holds still with the intrusion. She finds it very amusing that the perfection they apparently see in her is not the product of their scientific minipulations. With the close proximity, she gets a nose full of the other woman's scent. "**I'll be sure to let her know...**" Lexi's cyan gaze meets the Zabrak's. "**I must admit, I'm here for my own designs as well.**"
**"Of course, the same desires burn within all of your kind, Reaper."**
She doesn't seem to retreat, continuing to feel Lexi up and down.
**"Mmmnnn, superlative..."**

**"You know, standard Reaper Protocols require all of you to be subservient to every Scientist on this base, you are compelled to do whatever I desire... by your genetic programming. So... chooose your words carefully."** She gropes Lexi's butt.
"**I never said I was here because of desire,**" Lexi says lightly. "**I'm not built like the Reapers you've dealt with.**"
**"Clearly."** Stepping back she puckers her lips and puts a hand underneath her chin, **"So what is it you want of me."**
"**Your cooperation... if not your assistance,**" Lexi says. After a beat she adds: "**I need to check out a Remote Transceiver.**"
**"You don't have the clearance."**
With a shrug, Lexi indicates the solution is obvious. "**That's where you come in.**"
**"Let me think... I will help you, but only under one condition. I want you to let me analyze your genetic makeup, the next laboratory over has a DNA samplifier. Do we have a deal?"**
"**And why do you want my DNA?**" Lexi asks cautiously.
**"I want to Clone you and make some improvements, although... I must confess none come to mind at the moment."**

Lexi's expression remains nuetral as she considers the implications. She preferred to have the woman assist her willingly, but also having her DNA in the hands of one of the Reaper scientists was disconcerting. If she and Wraith had their way, there wouldn't be a cloning facility for it to matter. "**Alright. I'll let you take your sample.**"

**"Good Reaper, now come. Follow."** Her wrist alluringly twists in the air, as she gives Lexi a husky look and strolls past Veq, who stands dumbfounded and makes eye contact with Lexi, shrugging in exasperation.
*Sorry, this sometimes happens..."*
Is all he can whisper to Lexi as the Doctor has stepped out of the front door, and her heels can be heard clicking away.

Lexi steps up next to NZ and says quietly, "**I would like you to attempt to track where she stores the data... I don't intend on her keeping it if it can be helped.**" With a nod to both NZ and Veq she follows the doctor.
**"I shall do what I can Master."**
He follows them out, as the door shuts behind him leaving darkness.
Lexi picks up her pace to catch up with the doctor even though she knows she isn't going far. She trusts Veq and NZ to keep up with them as they move through the corridors.

The Doctor leads the heroic trio East, then turns South at the Central Turbolift, traveling along the main corridor South, and stops along a door to her left, swiping her White Keycard and entering inside. Another long narrow corridor winds like a snake, although, she doesn't need to go far, and swipes her access pass again, entering one of the doors along the left-hand side, entering Area 17. This appears to be a gymnasium, with massive *massive* weight machines for bench pressing and every other imaginable exercise. The gym currently stands empty. Strolling across nonchalantly, Gylvane enters Area 13, another empty area. This one seems to be built as a boxing arena, with an elevated section for the contestants, complete with elastic ropes. Bright lights illuminate the room. A jumbotron Scoreboard hangs from the ceiling, idling at the moment. As Lexi, Veq and N-Zee enter Area 13 and inspect the boxing rink, Doctor Gylvane stops and turns around looking at the three of them. **"Oh sorry, looks like I got lost..."** She shrugs, but somehow her words don't sound very sincere. The reason behind her tone becomes quickly apparent, as behind Lexi, *five* Reapers enter the room through the same door Lexi just used. All 5 are massive musclebound monsters, save for their leader - a sleek Sith with glowing violet eyes. Gylvane makes eye contact with the leader, and comes bounding over like a cheerleader, jumping into his arms and swinging around his neck, gracefully landing alongside and letting out a giggle, **"That's the Jedi I told you about, are you happy with me baby?"**
Lexi internally sighs as she realizes the scientist managed to spring the trap on them and they were now severly outnumbered.  "**What's this about, Doctor?**"

The leader seems to ignore Lexi, as he responds to Gylvane, who has also seemingly forgotten about Lexi, **"You have pleased me, Doctor. This Jedi shall be the perfect test subject for my newest crop of Mega-Reapers."**

Letting go of Doctor Gylvane, although she tries to cling a bit longer than proper, after prying her away, the leader turns to Lexi, Veq and N-Zee. **"Permit me to introduce myself. I am the Lead Researcher on this level, Chief Scientist, and overall Project Lead here at Tantis Base. Doctor Ul'dan. You, presumably, are the Jedi that has set off all the alarms blaring on this base, not letting us scientists focus on our magnificent work effort. It doesn't matter, however... in a way, you shall be aiding in our technological progress nonetheless. Either you fight my Projects one-on-one in that area there, pitting your best fighter against mine, until none remains, or I can call the Stormtroopers in right now to meet out justice. So, Jedi, what do you say."**
"**And when the fighting is done and I prevail... what then?**"
**"You won't, but... If you are arrogant enough to think that you can overpower my artistic genius with false bravado... fine. If you prevail, you are free to leave my laboratory alive."**
Sticks out a tongue, and pulls down one eyelid at Lexi.
**"Going down Jedi!"**

"**There's two issues. If you wish to put your... projects against me and my strongest allies, I'm afraid that they're not here with me. We were seperated, you see, as we made our way through the base. Wouldn't you want a comprehensive test?**"
**"Quit stalling, if you're too afraid to fight here and now, I can always just bring in our Imperial overseers to sort things out..."** He holds up a small device bristling with buttons.
**"Master, I'm sensing we have been double crossed."**
Groans.

Lexi glances over at Veq and NZ. Neither one of them had proven to be much of a match against the other Reapers they had come up against. There didn't seem to be much choice in the matter. Either she faces off against the four gruesome Reapers or contend with the base's security. "**Yes, NZ... Very astute.**" She turns back to Ul'dan. "**You give very little choice in the matter, which will be your first champion?**"

**"Splendid."** The doctor's voice is modulated by his mask and occasionally snaps octaves up or down at random intervals, interspersed with static. **"Now then, I shall bring out my first fighter..."** He turns to his cadre of monsters. **"Volunteers?"** There is a general hooting and hollering, some shoving and growling of nightmarishly hideous horrific monster noises. Eventually, after a quick scrimmage where the monsters pound each others faces, one of the monstrosities steps forward, appearing to be the victor. He has alien blood dripping down a maw full of nasty, wickedly sharp shark-like teeth. The creatures smiles. In the background, the doctor's voice intones: **"Qrel'gorath. An early prototype Mega-Reaper of mine. He seems eager to play with you... But how rude of me, why not let you each... introduce yourselves."**
**''~MONSTER GROWL~"**

While the Reapers fight with each other she steps up next to NZ to give him instructions. "**If you're able, attempt to escape here and make your way back to the others. Their orders are to rendeavous with Captain Kai's team if I don't contact them in an hour.**"

It's heavy footsteps pound up the steps to the boxing arena, where the other monsters already opened the elastic ropes permitting the first Mega-Reaper to enter the rink. It swings his large arms in windmills warming up. Cracking its neck back and forth, Qrel'gorath pounds his chest and lets out another monstrous bellow, which really grinds on the ears.
Watching the bizarre display, N-Zee nevertheless seems stoic to the whole affair, **"Yes, Commander. I think I'll let you distract them first, before making my move..."**
Lexi nods and turns back to the Reaper. "**What do you think, Veq? Are you up to the challenge?**"
**"I believe I can take him, yes, however I'm not exactly sure what this new body of mine can do yet..."**
**"I should like to try my organicidal program too, Master, I've recently been upgraded by my Maker with enhanced combat protocols."**

Slowly she shakes her head, keeping her voice low and turning her face away from the Reapers so they can't read her lips. "**You're the only one who may be able to get out of here if things turn against us. Veq, there's no time like the present to find out what you can do.**"

**"I won't let you down, Chosen One."** Throwing off his tattered, torn, and bloody cloak, the lanky reaper leaps up into the boxing rink, catching the ropes with both hands as he ascends, flipping into the arena. N-Zee's speaker grills play a trumpet meddley to maintain morale as Veq does so.
Veq ignites his purple blade, testing it against the lightsaber resistant ropes and finds them impervious to saber damage.
**"I must defeat you, all of you."**

A modulated mechanized laugh emanates, **"How amusing, our first fight will be Crab versus Shark. Who will prevail?"** He gestures as Doctor Gylvane has wheeled out a massive Gong. With a wind up, she strikes the gong with a mallot.

The fight begins as Veq'neviz charges headlong at the Mega-Reaper, violet weapon swinging back and forth wildly. As the shark-like Qrel swings his own massive cold-blue saber blade at Veq, the reborn Reaper's mind pulses pretenaturally at the attack, warning him of its incoming angle and speed, and Veq is able to duck beneath the attack. Qrel overbalances at the sudden miss, presenting his back to Veq. Veq takes the opportunity to swipe the Reaper's flanks twice with his blade, but alas he finds that his blades only find surface purchase against the sharkman's tough, thick rubbery hide, doing little more than decorative damage on the monster's outer nanodermis. Qrel turns around deliberately and smiles his shark-like smile. Veq gulps, as the creature is coming on again, backpedaling.
**"Let's go Sharky you're my man if you can't do it no one can!"**
**"Master Veq, might I suggest a lethal sneak attack to the genitals, I find that to be most effective against organics..."**

The monster seems to change tactics, extinguishing its lightsaber and replacing the saber at its belt. It winds up and forms a ramming pose, then charges at Veq like a bull, rebounding off the ropes and repeating this over and over again, not caring if a few spare blade slashes manage to slice at his tough hide-bound muscular arms. Veq, for his part, does surprisingly well against this tactic... recently having to read Xish's movements in a very similar fight not too long ago. Nevertheless, the continued glancing hits take their toll, leaving Veq mildly bruised by the whole affair and wheezing with exertion.

Closing his eyes, Veq shuts off all outside senses as the Mega-Reaper charges directly at him once more. Centering his soul, Veq places his violet blade in front of his face perpendicular to the ground and forms a meditative pose. At the last second, just as Qrel'gorath is about to bowl him over, Veq's crab-helm pulses with psychokinetic energy and he drops his center of gravity abruptly, making a lightning quick motion to slip beneath the monster's guard and wind up at its back. Several purple afterimages follow the transmuted alien Reaper. This time, however, instead of just slashing, Veq touches the Mega-Reaper with an outstretched hand, and releases a pulse of disruptive bioenergy. At first, this seemingly does nothing. The sharkman reels, turning around in a step, as if already expecting some kind of treachery. Qrel immediately grapples Veq in his two massive tree-trunk arms, crushing Veq in a titanic grip - we hear crunching and splintering of bone as Veq screams out. Qrel slams Veq on the ground, and stomps him for good measure with one foot. However, just then, the biopulse takes effect... as Qrel's muscles seem to wiggle and seize up, twisting the Mega-Reaper painfully against his will into a humanoid pretzel. Monstrous gasps and cries can be heard as Qrel's own body pops, crunches, and twists, grinding and damaging itself as its own muscles are turned back against it. In the end, a gruesome puddle of blue blood, muscles, pink viscera, and bulging eyeballs is left on the floor. Qrel's hearts have both been smothered and crushed, letting out a wet fart of defeat. Veq weakly stands up, a broken being, alive but his world full of fire and pain, wheezing worse than ever, bubbling noises coming out of his crab-helm, dripping purple liquid.
**"Sushi is served."**
**"How disappointing... Clean up isle 1."** A couple of the other monsters enter the ring and begin devouring Qrel's remaining viscera.

Lexi motions Veq to come towards her, eyeing his wounds with concern. "**You did well. Do you have any of those bacta injectors?**" She steps up into the ring, intending to take on the next fight so Veq has an opportunity to recover.
Nodding weakly, he collapses against a wall and slides down, groaning, **"In my cloak, there is a bandolier, its got recently stocked ~wince~ injectors."**
**"Well well, what have we here. Decided to take your leave did you, droid?"**
N-Zee has been hoisted up by one of the monstrosities, wiggling his legs uselessly.
**"I suggest you remain where you are, the fun has yet to begin."** N-Zee gets tossed against the wall, immediately next to Veq.
Lexi goes and retrieves Veq's cloak with the bandolier, handing it over to him. Lexi glances around, and sees NZ being hoisted up having failed to make his way out of the training room undected.
**"I'm sorry, Master, but they must have infra-red sensors, should have realized that sooner."**

Lexi nods. She doesn't blame the droid at all for the failure. "**It was worth the attempt.**" She places a hand on Veq's shoulder as she stands and returns to the ring, taking in a deep calming breath as she surveys the grisly scene of the other Reapers consuming the loser.

The doctor's mechanically warped laughter chills Lexi to the bone. **"Suddenly your pathetic little team isn't looking so healthy. Shall we finally witness that vaunted Jedi combat prowess at long last?"**

In the center of the rink, the three remaining monsters were busy fighting each other for the leanest scraps. Burping and patting their bellies, they look with hateful glares at the other monsters as if eyeing up who would be next to step up for their team. Finally, there seems to be an unspoken agreement reached and... The bat-faced albino Mega-Reaper hisses at Lexi in response to her challenge.
**"HSSSSSS-- Thissss one issss Vappppthhhh, Marog. Your heart shall sssssoon be rotting insssssside my.... sssstomachssss...."***
The creature ignites a crimson lightwhip.
**"Seems we have our second match, Vapth'marog versus... I nearly forgot to ask, what is your name Jedi?"**

Lexi eyes the Reaper with his unusual weapon warily, aware the whip gives her opponent the advantage of reach. "**Jedi is fine,**" she responds, her dual blades igniting. The green and yellow a stark contrast to the red whip of her opponent.

Lexi charges the monster, getting in close to avoid the whip, attacking with her sabers. Her body nearly swoons and threatens to faint, eyes watering she can still see the evil Mega-Reaper smiling with its deformed bat-like face. Lexi gets an upclose view of a hideous, shattered and bleeding face, snarling with malice. Lexi's yellow blade ignites and it cuts through Vapth's hand doing minimal damage, but drawing away the monster's attention for a split second, long enough for Lexi to slip down and out of its grasp. Lexi whirls around and extends her flat palm into the center of the monster's chest. Vapth'marog flies out of the arena, with a wet splat impacting the far wall and forming a star of gory pink blood, sticking where he landed arms and legs outsplayed, like a sideways crime scene.

Screwing up his ear, Ul'dan breaks the tense silence. **"At least, we'll never hear that discordant sound again... Well done! You acquitted yourself well, Jedi. How are your broken ribs feeling? Lexi's breath heaves painfully as she straightens, her sabers turning off and she puts them, albiet temporarily at her belt. Taking one deep, painful breath, she focuses on the Verdant Force in an effort to restore her vitality and ease the burning pain in her chest.

Lexi jumps down from the rink and joins her companions, her ribs feeling better after using her ability to heal them. "**It's the unfortunate circumstance we've found ourselves in. **"More progress is needed it seems, I'd like to thank you for this combat test of my Mega-Reaper's, Jedi. "**There is more to life than survival. It's sad to see you've lost sight of such things... I wish I could say I pity you, but I have seen the horrors created here in the name of science. I pity them instead,**" she vaguely gestures towards the last of the Reapers.

**"~MONSTEROUS BELLOWING HOWL~"**

Two Mega-Reapers fly headlong into each other, Qril and Illox splatter together into a meat pie, though oddly - they stick together like two clay statues slowly fusing together, blending blue and tan colors. Lexi breaths heavily as she watches the last of the golem parts disperse. Slowly she turns to face the two scientists as her breathing evens out. The female Reaper scientist is nowhere to be seen, having disappeared at some point during the match.
The Chief scientist claps slowly, **"Splendid, well done, bravo."**
He hops into the boxing ring, igniting two violet blades.

Lexi is not surprised by the development having expected he would not let them go. "**Gylvane decide not to stay to cheerlead you?**" Lexi asks, noticing the woman was now gone.

Uldan begins helicoptering his blades slowly, two lazy windmills spreading the Poison all around the room.

Lexi eyes the growing cloud of gas and takes a final good breath of air. She doesn't waste her breath in a retort as she watches him warily and ignites her green lightsaber.

The gas cloud parts, as only thanks to Lexi's Verdant Sight is she able to anticipate and block the incoming volley to attacks from Uldan, who strikes with perfectly fast slashes that are strangely powerful given the Doctor's light build, and Lexi begins sense something about his form isn't natural. The remaining plants attempt to surround Uldan, but he is able to slip out of them nimbly, taking a few scratches by the thorns.

A perfect attack slices a deep gash into Lexi, almost eliciting her to open her mouth in a scream. **"Don't worry, I need you alive... for now."**
Dual wrist ion blasters flare out through the purple haze.

With the latest slash of his lightsabers, the pain from the wounds she's sustained nearly becomes unbearable. Centering herself she calls on her inherrant ability to heal through the Verdant Force allowing it to steady her before she's once more on the defensive.
One blaster does reach Lexi, eliciting her muscles to tremble and nearly drop her weapons.

Lexi sees the Doctor power down his sabers and fill a syringe with some kind of drug: **"Hold still..."** His spectre approaches inside of the purple haze. Lexi's vision swims as she gets pumped full of a powerful sedative.

Lexi awakens a short while later, finding herself in a small rectangular glass-walled room. This glass prison is located inside of a much larger laboratory. Lexi blinks in the sudden brightness of the room, her thoughts muddied at first by the lack of air and then the drugs she had been subjected to. Three more massive monsters step up at the Doctor's side.

Ghost is picking up Veq, helping the alien stand.

Lexi had tensed as the group of scout troopers round the corner, but she senses the mental minipulation that ends up disguising them to the squad's senses. Her concern grows for their companion as the effort sends him into unconciousness. "**Let's get him to the medbay quickly,**" she urges.

After 15 minutes of rest, everyone has been brought back to full health thanks to the highly advanced medical technology here, which is state of the art. The only one still out of commission is NZ.

Lexi is stunned for a moment as Magnet's words wash over her and for a moment her heart seems to stop. With a sudden intake of breath she comes back to the present.

"**I have the ability to freeze the AT-ST for a short time.  Possibly long enough for you to overwhelm the troopers so we can then deal with the AT-ST,**"  She frowns as she considers how difficult even the coordination for such a maneuver would be. **"I see, well so far your record hasn't inspired me with competence. Nevertheless, in for a microcredit... in for a cubit..."**

"**It's no easy task to infiltrate a den of vipers and attempt to assassinate a heavily guarded target with little to no resources,**" she remarks dryly. "**Considering I'm still alive to make the effort should at least inspire something.**" Glad to see NZ now fully operational she nods, satisfied.  "**We were unable to secure Gylvane to use her as my disguise.  Now we must bypass the security using more aggressive methods.**"

**"You knew what you were getting walking in here... Nevertheless, as I said, I am still for helping you accomplish this difficult task. Do you not possess Xass' security card already? **"You realize if an investigaiton is done, that little slip could be traced back to me... but... **"The Emperor and his closest family members."**
"**And how many... relations does he have?**"
**"I'm not the one to ask about this, all I know is what I've overheard in passing."**
"**It's more than what I've heard,**" she says in an effort to entice him to say more. "**There's no more than the Reaper Queen here?**"

We shall have a *friendly contest* between our respective Departments... Of course, in such a contest, accidents *do happen*. It would be a shame, but if one of us were to die in due course of such a duel, then it would be written off just as another 'workplace accident' wouldn't it. How unfortunate for you hyuk hyuk hyuk."** Spittle was flying from her lips and her face was blue as her aura was positively irradiated with rage.
**"Yes, Grand Inquisitor Yaga, it shall be as you say... forgive my intrusion..."**
**"Doctor. You and your mouthy Reaper just signed your death warrants."**
**"Don't count your hatchlings before they are birthed, you gimp. I'll wipe the floor with your intestines when we're through."**
"**We shall see...**" Lexi says slowly, her eyes narrowed. She steps up next close to Zyelee, turning her head so the Inquisitors can't see her lips. "**You or me first?**"
**"I want first crack."**

She nods at Zyelle and turns back to Yaga. "**My Reaper has accepted your challenge, *Grand Inquisitor*,**" Lexi says, saying the title mockingly, if only to serve to continue to goad her. Perhaps she'd become consumed by the Dark Side enough, the Ysalmiri would affect her.

**"Your *Reaper* is nothing but a test-tube baby with breasts manyfold larger than her brain. *It* is not worthy of speaking to me, much less engaging in mortal combat with me on any equal playing field. For your *Creature* I shall match my student... Quinth, step forward. My honor has been offended. I wish to see this bimbo broken before me."**
A new Inquisitor steps forward, igniting a red blade. He has red goggles and some kind of augmented robotic limbs on his backpack.
**"You heard the boss, get ready to die, Abdomination. First you... then I shall taste Jedi flesh on my interrogation scalpel..."**
The arms whirr and a flurry of virboscalpels begin vibrating.
Zyelle cracks her neck back and forth, stretching her fingers and cracking them at the same time.
Lexi steps to the side and away from the band of Inquisitors under the guise of giving the duelers room.
**"Bring it, virgin. Let's hope you last longer than you do against your hand."**
**"Contestants... Take your places. First to disable the other opponent permanently is the victor... And... Begin."**

The sparring arena had a clearly demarcated perimeter. The non-participants took position on the elevated bridge ringing the arena, as Quinth dropped down, grinning at the sexy Reaper and clearly looking forward to dissecting her tender flesh. He licked his lips obscenely.
**"I promise not to be gentle..."**

The Inquisitor opened up with a jetpack-aided launch, flying towards Zyelle. His vibroscalpels were vibrating so loudly, everyone's ears experienced tinnitus and the air around him was a blur of steel.

Zyelle took her stance, powering up, her hair began to float partially in the air as she twisted and began her triple-slice technique, a beautiful dance that perfectly dismantled the lightning fast attack from the Inquisitor, so fast that the human eye could not keep up.

Quinth gurgled out an incoherent gasp, blood welling up in his throat and spilling down his chin. Standing still, his armor components popped off one after another, then his chest armor unzipped, and his torso fell apart into perfectly chopped cube chunks. Nothing was left but a pile of meat cubes on the floor.
**"Oops, I guess he didn't last long after all... Hooo hooo~"**
Stumbling, she fell on one knee, all of a sudden.
A small line of blood had been drawn by one of Quinth's scalpels. Unbeknownst to Zyelle, his scalpels were poisoned with an overpowering neuro-toxin, capable of effecting even Force users.

**"Hoo hoo indeed... Seems your Reaper didn't last very long either, Doctor. The poison will kill her soon. Watch how she hyperventilates even now... Soon the carbon dioxide poisoning will go to her brain and she'll expire... but not before she *suffers* for her words to me."**
Zyelle's chest was heaving with accelerated gasps, and her expression was one of sadness. She looked into Lexi's eyes, unable to speak, and fainted to the lawn.
Lexi was left standing alone, six Inquisitors eyeing her down with hunger in their glowing eyes.

Lexi purses her lips and looks at the Inquisitors one by one. With Zyelle she would have had a chance, but now it seemed she was alone. She also had the means to save the Reaper, but wasn't sure she'd have the time to do so with the Inquisitors eyeing her like their next meal.

Suddenly, we see Zyelle rise up weakly on both arms and get up. She grins, pointing to her forearm. The line of blood where there was a scalpel slice has disappeared, and instead there is a small slice along her vambrace, which doesn't pierce the skin. **"Thanks for telling me your lackey's trick, Granny. Too bad I have a trick of my own, to match and exceed yours."** She brushes the dust off her shoulder in an exaggerated arrogant motion. Lexi can sense though, that Zyelle is still somewhat drained from the last fight, breasts heaving with exertion.
Yaga's upper lip curls into a snarl. **"Arrogant whore."**
Zyelle gives an exaggerated sly wink.
**"You are clearly in no condition to fight, whore. Why not let your Jedi-masquerading-as-scientist try to survive my next subordinate. What say you, Jedi?"**

Lexi is relieved as Zyelle suddenly recovers, and wonders, not for the first time, what exactly the Reapers ability allowed her to do. Alter time perhaps? It was going to continue to be a mystery as the Grand Inquisitor initiates the next round of fighting. It reminded her once more of the fights she had to endure against Ul'dan and his Reapers and she gets the sense there would be no winning this one either. With slow deliberation, Lexi removes the white lab coat and gently tosses it over a nearby ledge. There was no getting around the conflict she could see, especially while she was unsure whether the squad had managed to free Bishop yet. Distracting the group of Inquisitors would have to be her contribtion. "**Are you afraid that I'll beat you. Why not face me yourself?**" Lexi taunts as she withdraws her green blade from within the pocket dimension and her yellow from her belt. She ignites them, pointing them down and to the side as if the threat the half dozen Inquisitors pose don't concern her in the slightest.

**"You wouldn't survive fighting me Jedi... I want you alive long enough to torture from you the location of the Jedi enclave you came from. No, I think I'll kill you later, on my torture table, at my leisure. For now, I shall amuse myself watching that fighting spirit inside you fail gradually as my students wear you down, tear you down, and humiliate you in every way imaginable for my amusement."**

She snaps her fingers. **"Yutra. Teach her the meaning of pain."** The female Inquisitor jumps down into the arena, nimbly doing a double flip. She is nimble and takes off her mask revealing a pale, near-vampiric face with glowing eyes.
Her red saber seems to orbit the new Inquisitor. **"Jedi... how I have missed torturing your kind..."**

Lexi brings her sabers up into a classic defensive stance as she watches the Inquisitor and her floating blade carefully. "**You must introduce me to your surgeon then... you look better than the old hag.**"
**"I was once a Jedi myself, until Inquisitor Yaga showed me the truth of the Dark Side. Perhaps I can now, share that truth with you."**

**"You remind me of myself when I was young and naive, and thought the world was full of wonderful people. Then those wonderful people betrayed me, when the Empire came calling for my village and family. They all turned me over immediately, to save their own sorry souls. The joke's on them... after I became an Inquisitor I returned to my home village and burned it to the ground."**
Yutra seems to walk a slow leisurely circle around Lexi's periphery, red saber slowly swinging threateningly in the air as she speaks. Her words are venomous and filled with barely restrained malice.
**"I revelled in their screams for mercy, and watched the flames devour them before my very eyes."**

Lexi follows the Inquisitors path through the Force, her body remaining in position and her mind centering as she listens to her tale. There were parallels to their stories: both of them were handed over to another order, though her village had done so with hope that one day Lexi would return and further their prosperity. Instead of the anger she imagined Yutra was trying illicit from the young Jedi Knight, she instead felt pity. Yutra likely had never received Jedi training, but because of her affinity to the Force, had been singled out as a sacrifial lamb. Instead of it becoming a gift she could share with her village, it had turned her into a destroyer.
"**I imagine your story is the same for many like you,**" Lexi comments evenly.
Yaga smiles evily, nodding along.
**"I learned to harness my hatred, and those of others, bringing it forth, cultivating it, allowing it to bubble up..."**

Yutra seems to control her lightsaber with the left hand, gracefully manipulating the blade telekinetically. Meanwhile, with her right hand she begins to trace a lazy circle. Black liquid begins to coagulate in the air by the palm of her right hand. The substance is oily and dark, Lexi can feel it consists of concentrated Hatred.

Smiling, Yutra reveals a row of sharpened mutilated dagger like teeth. This is at great odds with her amazingly beautiful body, and Lexi feels a pang of pity hit her guts. The black bubbling liquid has built up into the size of a basketball, sloshing around like a squirming evil bubbling cauldron of concentrated Evil. The Inquisitor pulls back her hand and shoves the liquid forward, shooting it out like a water cannon. At the same time with her off hand, the lightsaber swings once and ignites the Enmitic Liquid, causing it to transform into a jet of orange flame, lighting up the arena as it spouts in Lexi's direction.
Lexi is able to hold out one hand and form a green shield bubble, as the napalm splashes against the Force Shield created by the Jedi.

Lexi meanwhile winds up her own Force power and brings out her palm forward in response, hitting Yutra with a massively powerful gust of Force Push. Hitting Yutra dead center, the Inquisitor seems to fly backwards at great speed, but just as suddenly she seems to stop herself mid flight and levitate in response, not receiving any impact damage. She smiles her shark teeth at Lexi, bubbles of Enmitic Oil coalescing once more and beginning to rotate around her center of mass like miniature planets or moons.

Yutra chucks one of the Fireball at Lexi, who leaps over it and charges the Inquisitor with both blades leading. The evil fire witch tries to levitate out of the way, but she isn't as fast a Lexi, who gets two great slices in. They would have hurt a lot more, had the witch not also summoned a red force bubble of her own to blunt the attacks largely negating them, but nevertheless Lexi does score two small hits - the first of the match.
**"Yessss... Glorious Pain! I feel it, so wonderful and raw and full of emotion!"**
**"Don't get carried away Yutra... You have a job to do, don't forget..."**
Yutra is laughing madly and hurling Fireballs willy nilly. Lexi's focus narrows and she is able to block with a green shield bubble.

At first her aim is terrible, but after a while there are so many explosives that Lexi feels as if she just stepped into a warzone. Eventually, one of the Fireballs finds its mark, from sheer luck. The Enmitic Fireball hits Lexi and burns her True Jedi Robes, however, something unexpected happens.

Lexi's aura seems to take all the bite out of the Enmitic Fireball. Instead of Corrupting her Jedi Soul, like Yutra had hoped, she finds a massive reserve of Serenity and Kindness. Yutra is so taken aback by this, that she rears back in surprise. Putting both hands over her face, she begins to sob.

Lexi pauses as the woman's demeanor suddenly changes. Standing over Yutra, she turns off her green saber as she comes in closer to speak to her in low, soft tones. "**You have known nothing but anger and pain for your life. It's been such a heavy burden for you; staying ever in the darkness. It's not to late... to let it all go.**"

Yutra seems stunned, her face goes slack and she looks like a trauma victim as she simply slumps down on the ground sobbing. At Lexi's words she begins to wail and weep openly, sobbing into Lexi's arms.
**"DIE, WEAKLING--"** A heavy blaster rifle opens up on Yutra from above.
Lexi ignites her blades and deflects both bolts.

Lexi was patting Yutra's back comfortingly when she sensed the coming danger. With her yellow blade still in hand having expected some sort of retaliation from the observing pack of Sith, she moves quickly into action. The bolts from above were easily bat aside and redirected harmlessly away from her and the defenseless Inquisitor at her feet. Not waiting for another retaliatory volley, Lexi reaches down to pull Yutra to her feet and back her up towards Zyelle. "**I understand you've had a bit of a shock... but you need to snap out of it if you're going to survive this!**"
**"Get away Jedi!"** She shoves Lexi away, standing up and wiping her tears from her face. **"I can care for myself."**
**"Seems as though your 'friends' betrayed you. Wanna join our side instead?"**
**"I'm on my own side. However... In a fight, I won't attack you."**
Zyelle shrugs. **"Good enough I guess."**

Lexi isn't phased by the rebuff and let's Yutra figure out her own way. Instead, she looks up at the five remaining Inquisitors. "**Our odds are better working together than on our own,**" she says glancing over at Yutra.

Yutra casts an evil glare at Lexi, though has a distinctly less heavy impression in the Force to Lexi's senses: **"Don't make me regret my decision, Jedi. You won't be able to win without my foreknowledge of the Inquisitor's abilities. You need me, more than I need you. Understand?"**
On the elevated walkway, the 5 Sith are all unveiling their hoods and discarding their cloaks, drawing out a credre of red lightsabers.
Yutra sheds her Sith robes, throwing them with disgust on the ground, revealing a rocking body.

Lexi inclines her head in acknowledgement of the statement. '**You better explain quickly, as it appears this is about to become a melee.**" Casually, she glances back where Veq has been hiding all this time.
Choosing to remain as backup, Veq receives Lexi's telepathic touch, and imparts a comforting sense of reassurance, followed by questioning sense of whether it was time to rejoin the other combatants.

Lexi gives a negative in response to his probe and turns her attention back to the Inquisitors. She ignites her green blade to join her yellow and holds them once more out to the side in a ready stance as she observers. "**Give us the short notes, Yutra.**"

The first of the Inquisitors drops down to the arena, eyeing the trio of ladies with unconcealed perverted malice. Yutra narrates quietly as he enters the rink: **"That's Inquisitor Skarn. He can summon chains."** Lexi realizes that Veq can listen to their conversation telepathically also, gathering intelligence.
The giant walking tank drops down next, causing a massive BOOM and mini earthquake. Yurla continues to narrate: **"You've already met Inquisitor Drax. He's more machine than man. Ware his claws."**

Floating down to the arena on a Floating Disk of pure Red Energy, arms folded behind his back is a thin hook nosed Inquisitor. **"That's Zoldo. He has the power of Image Projection, including Psychokinetic Clones. The images *can* harm you."**

Next, the pompous Inquisitor holding the maliciously evil Sith tome, smoothly glides down to hover gracefully next to his compatriots, a few centimeters above the ground, netherworlds winds whipping his hair disconcertingly. Yutra whispers harshly: **"That's Inquisitor Zern. He summons Dark Side creatures and casts other Sith Sorcery."**

Lastly, Grand Inquisitor Yaga, remaining on the elevated balcony smirks as she is content to seemingly watch the proceedings without interference. Yutra looks up at her former Master, **"Grand Inquisitor Yaga. She has power over Curses and Hexes. That's all I now."**
The Grand Inquisitor seems to calmly stand and observe the melee from above, folding her arms behind her back and smirking.
**"So... it looks like 4 on 4. Granny wants to watch. Who wants to fight whom?"**
**"I can take any of them. Wait... who is the fourth on our team?"**
**"That would be me, Dark Lady."** The earth quakes as a transformed, bulkier and more armored version of Veq steps out from the foliage.
Lexi looks over the Inquisitors entering the ring. "**We didn't come completely unprepared,**" she says slowly. "**Veq. Zyelle. I'll let you two pick first.**"

Stomping up to the three ladies, Veq regards the arrayed menu of Inquisitors before him. The enemy Inquisitors have stopped at this newest appearance and taken pause at the new Charybdis Creature entering the arena. Very soon it becomes evident that the only Inquisitor to match Veq's mass is Drax, and the two immediately lock gazes, beginning a staredown. Veq cracks his knuckles at Drax, then ignites his violet blade, as if testing his new nanotech-enhanced muscular arms, swinging his lightsaber, checking its reach. Drax is breathing hard and grunting like an overheating engine, eyes glowing red with hatred back at Veq, igniting red triple saber-claws in response from one of his gauntlets. He then retracts the outer two red blades, giving Veq the middle finger. The other Inquisitors laugh in a relaxed manner at the insult, but Lexi can sense they are a bit nervous and using humor to cover up their suprise at this newest combatant. Veq nods towards Drax: **"I shall dispose of that one."**
"**He's all yours,**" Lexi says lightly. "**Zyelle, what do you think about Zoldo?**"
**"I'll gladly eviscerate the little hook nosed nerd if you want me to Jedi."**
"**I'll take Skarn then,**" Lexi eyes the Inquisitor who had entered the arena first.
**"Very well, so you're leaving me to deal with Zern's Sorceries. Makes sense. If I burn that spell book to a crisp, then that's the end of his magiks."**

At that pronouncement, the opponents on each side lock gazes and begin studying the opposition. On the opposite side the arena, the Inquisitor see as Lexi's team is getting into formation and eyeing down each of them in turn, they realize instinctively who has been matched against whom.

It made sense to match Yutra against Zern, who clearly had the power of flight. It wasn't clear how Zyelle was planning to even reach Zoldo, if he simply chose to drift upward on his floating red disk, but she seemed to have some kind of plan in mind at least.

Skarn seemed to be the only other non-flying Inquisitor apart from Drax, and one Lexi could at least go toe to toe against, given her own martial skill. She had matched against Garathrax and even beat him in a straight up fight, but it wasn't clear what tricks this newest fanatic had. These Inquisitors had the advantage of experience on their side. Unlike raw fresh Reapers, they had survived more battles and fought through multiple new scenariors, making them much more seasoned warriors than manufactured Reapers were. The stakes were higher than ever. Lexi's quick thinking and relatively abrupt recruitment of Yutra had somehow evened the odds up at the last minute. Now it was up to her new Team to execute the plan. With four lethal fights coming up ahead, Lexi couldn't be sure who would survive and who would be forever ended. This could be the last conversation she was having with Veq, or Zyelle, or Yutra... or with anyone for that matter.

**"Veq's appearance seemed to take all the juice out of their balls. Maybe now they would be willing to fight duels one on one? Or we could just rush them all now and have an all out War right here right now between two sides."** Cracks her neck. **"Either works for me."**

As the two sides begin to slowly advance on each other, closing the distance, Lexi begins to hear the voices of the Inquisitors as they are speaking to each other. Although most of their conversation revolves around torture, pain, suffering, and their undying hatred for the Jedi, there are also a few tactical notes here and there thrown in.

"**Let's get this over quickly... We have things to do,**" Lexi remarks quietly. She takes a deep breath to center herself and not for the first time recites a mantra to herself. They were fighting for their lives. Her focus narrows on her chosen adversary and she brandishes her lightsabers and brings them up into a traditional defensive stance as she observes him.

Veq's helm pulses as he enacts Telepathy, explaining this to his fellow teammates: *I sense they're setting up a telepathic nexus to coordinate their attacks. We should do the same on our side. If you've never been this type of fight before, its important to let your team mates know when you're in trouble, and efficiently switch up opponents - coordinating to make sure the enemy never has a chance to act two-against-one.*
*Hrmph. Try not to bother me with your psychic chat room, I don't like to be interrupted while I work.*
*The corollary being, if we can coordinate a quick switch-up, we might be able to end one of them while their back is turned. I thought you would be thrilled at that fact, Emo Tits.*
Drax seems to huff and puff, accelerating his walk into a jog into a full on run. Veq begins pumping his arms and legs as he also begins to run headlong at Drax.
Both teams run at each other full speed, yelling and swinging their blades as they jump together meeting in the middle.

The grey-skinned vampiric Inquisitor with massive muscles comes in lightning fast blade leading. Lexi is somehow able to use her years of Jedi training to use one blade to skillfully change the direction of his red blade, though he still scores a glancing cut, while Lexi's other hand is able to weave a perfect counterstrike drawing a deep wound along Skarn's flank. Instead of letting out a howl of pain, or even flinching, you hear the sibilant sounds of extasy as Skarn lets the wound bake into his flesh, relishing its feel. What's more, his insides seem to glow orange and you can see his orange insides breathing up and down, having opened up a display window where there was previously none. The Inquisitor seems not to mind, vibrating madly as he lets the pain enter his body.

The two saber specialists begin a lightning quick exchange of blows in the middle of the arena, neither taking a step back. Their blades move so fast that to the outside eye, it looks like a high speed rave is happenning center ground.

Veq and Drax collide headon in a massive crack and thunderclap, splitting the earth as they each slam into one another, rebounding but not falling. The impact seems to jar both combatants. Veq had deflected Drax' lightsaber-claws with his tough armor. Likewise Drax seemed not to much damage from Veq's violet blade. Both realizing the other's armor was near-impervious to blades, they both closed down their weapons and began grappling each other. Slow, ponderous wrestling moves were brought out on each side, slams and piledrivers, arm holds and hip twists.

After a crazy exchange, involving a set up and then an elbow to Drax' forehead landing square on, Veq wins the initial exchange in skillful fashion. Seems that Veq's body was so powerful that it transcended the metallic cyborg opponent. After all, robots aren't exactly known for their graceful agility. However, the ability of Veq's organic flesh to bend and twist gave him a massive advantage here.

Drax roars an electronic roar in deviance, shaking his head and breathing hard. He seems unphased, despite taking a savage beating. His deep, electronic voice laughs an evil laugh and he comes on again at Veq, even more energized than before - enraged, Lexi could feel his Dark Side Force Signature growing as the fight went on. At this rate, the raging cyborg could work himself up into a frenzy enough to crack open even her crab-headed ally.

content: Zyelle had expertly and nimbly hipped and skipped across the many red, glowing objects that were beginning to appear in her path: red energy cubes, red energy cones, red energy scimitars, whirling disks and other Psychokinetic Projections were all used as skipping stones to the agile Reaper. Using them to gain air time, she began rotating her body around its axis in a whirling attack at Zoldo.

Zoldo was seemingly unconcerned, he hadn't even bothered to uncross his arms from behind his back. Calmly elevating his position, he put himself outside of the reach of the sexy Reaper. His face was flat and emotionless. This however had the unintended consequence of pulling him away from the group melee, though in the upward direction - where he could get even more vantage to attack from.

One of Zoldo's red energy scimitars does land a slice on Zyelle's exposed tush, drawing a small gasp of pain from the Reaper, but luckily it was a very shallow odd-angle cut, as she managed to use her incredible agility to veer away from the slow attack. Had she been human, she would have been bisected at the waist. She should have seen it coming, but the fact that Zoldo could attack her blind spot, that he could summon and unsummon objects from thin air with the simple application of his mind, gave him an advantage she realized.

Zoldo all of a sudden gasped in pain, coughing up blood, as somehow a new slash had materialized across his collarbone. Snarling his hook nose turned towards Zyelle and eyes glowed evily red. A large vein throbbed prominently next to his eye, one lip curling in rage.

Inquisitor Zern is flying at high speed hurling spells. Green magik energy is emanating from his eyes as evil gremlins appear from areas where he points his fingertips. In his off hand he holds a desiccated book, incanting evil ancient Sith words from his spell tome. Its pages flapping evily in the wind, turning as if of their own volition to exactly the page he needs.

The Ex-Inquisitor is keeping up the manic flight around the area with her own levitative powers. She floats around like the fire witch she is, easily dodging the small critters as they are way too slow to swipe at her with their fingernails and knashing teeth without getting anywhere close. The only thing Zern's attacks do is make her mad, helping her to cast her Enmitic Fireballs. One by one she sends them in Zern's direction, using her saber to remember to ignite them just before launch.

Meanwhile, at center field, Lexi is dancing a deadly dance against Inquisitor Skarn. Whirling her body 360 degrees she brings out the flat of her palm into the surprised Sado-Masochist's exposed raw wound. The massive Force blast sends him pinwheeling into a wall, comically beneath Grand Inquisitor Yaga. She watches Lexi's eyes with hatred, not bothering to look down at Skarn. Skarn has formed a dent into the ferrocrete wall, though the massive musclebound champion seems to treat it as if the material were styrofoamite. Grinning, he stands up from it as though from a bath and shakes off the rocks.  Lexi can tell he tried to intact some type of Sith Sorcery, but it seems as though she got off her spell first.
Drax is continuing to Wrestle against Veq, going at it full force, and workig himself up further and further into a rage.

content: Veq continues to deliver punishing back-cracking moves, elbow slams, and armbars, utterly devastating Drax again and again. However, the cyborg is mostly metal and doesn't feel hardly any of it, but enough to where his servomotors are beginning to smoke and whine a bit.
A heavy breathing imparts Lexi's mind, as Zyelle is dodging and weaving away from Telekinetic Weapons of all kinds: *~huff huff!~ This fucking nerd, is pissing me off. He won't stand and fight!*

Lexi backs up from her confrontation with Skarn and risks shifting her gaze towards Zoldo as he flies higher above the battlefield. There was no way Zyelle would effectively be able to reach him, but Lexi could using her telekinitic skills with the Force. *Switch. Take Skarn,* she responds simply.

*Thanks! I'm counting on you~* Ducking her head, she sprints away from Zoldo, weaving fast between the other combatants, confusing Zoldo's targeting - the hook nosed Inquisitor sneers with rage as he has to hold back summoning more psychokinetic weapons, lest he accidentally scure Zern or Drax or Skarn.
Zyelle executes her Signature Triple Threat, spinning like a balerina. The last hit slices Skarn at the shoulder, eliciting more sibilant pleasure noises and chortling.

Meanwhile, across the battlefield Inquisitor Zern's eyes flare green as he finishes shouting an evil incantation, his voice ending in a crescendo. A green summoning circle on the ground glows brightly, and in an explosion of green flame a massive Demonic Ursa bursts from the ground.

Yutra continues her onslaught with three more Enmitic Fireballs, two of which find their mark despite Zern's weak last second attempt to summon a Force Shield. It seems he was drained from Summoning his minion, which was charging into the giant-fight between Veq and Drax, tackling Veq.

Veq takes the new opponent in stride, even though the Ursa's brute strength is powerfully strong, Veq's skill and incredible endurance combine to make this fight nearly unfair for the Dark Summon. Veq knees the bear in the face, breaking it's left fang and follow it up with a piledriver as he swings around the Ursa's left shoulder like a handlebar and twists it, landing with an elbow to the back of the bear's head. Its face bites into the ground, tearing out perfect pristine park lawn and cultivated flowers.

Meanwhile, across the field, Zyelle is executing another perfect Triple Threat - two slices land, including across Skarn's head, leaving part of his braincase exposed and glowing yellow. For his part, the Inquisitor just laughs maniacally, black smoke billowing from his body as his aura of Evil becomes so palpable its now becoming visible to the naked eye. No matter how much punishment this Inquisitor takes, he seems utterly unstoppable. He stabs with his saber twice, swings so wild they should by all rights miss, but he seems to anticipate Zyelle's foot work and score a painful gash across her sexy torso. Though Zyelle is dealing out punishing slashes, she doesn't seem nearly as durable as the Inquisitor's strange evil flesh. Her attacks hurt, but she doesn't know if she can stand the attrition which is continuing to be dished out by the vampiric champion.

Lexi nimbly opens her Dimensional Pocket and catches a thermal detonator in her waiting palm. Using the Force to hurl it up, up, and up - all the way to Inquisitor Zoldo. The hook nosed telekine had felt some kind of attack coming, and had enacted his best Force Shield to date: forming a near impenetrable red sphere of energy. Nevertheless, as his eyes resolved the object hurtling at him he betrayed the first hint of emotion -- eyes going wide as he witnesses the grenade impact against his Safety Bubble and rock his world, cracking it apart in a massive explosion, some of the force of the blast hitting his ear and breaking his concentration on the Small Army of Telekinetic Weapons dancing on the ground attempting to slice Lexi to ribbons, as she also enacts her own Green Barrier, fortunately its just barely strong enough that Zoldo's Telekinetic Weapons all just gaggle up uselessly clattering against her shields.

The combat ensuing in the designated arena was nothing more than pandemonium has Force Users fought with lightsabers, Force abilities and brute strength. It amazes Lexi for just a moment that such sight wouldn’t draw station security as the combatants one up each other in their attempts to eliminate their opponents. 
Switching from the more martial combatant Skarn, allowing Zyelle to take her place as the Inquisitor rises from where Lexi had flung him, Lexi looks up at the hovering Inquisitor Zoldo, an idea springs to mind. Lexi by all means is not a master of the telekinetic arts, but that doesn’t mean she doesn’t have a few tricks up her sleeve. Or in this case; a pocket. Focusing on the pocket at her breast, she withdraws one of the few remaining thermal detonators she had brought with her from the Antioch and hurls it up, pushing it onwards with Force given speed. Meeting its mark, she’s only mildly disappointed as the Inquisitor raises his own shield, protecting himself from the majority of the blast. However, his reaction perhaps was worth the effort. It was a trick she likely would only be able to use once, but now the Inquisitor knows she isn’t without resources.
Her thoughts are diverted as she instinctively realizes danger is charging towards her. Warned by the Force she manages to block the first of the burly Inquisitor’s Saberclaws and returns the attack with one of her own, but his second swipe leaves a gash of seared flesh along her side as she leaves herself open. 
Now faced with two enemies, she reaches out into the link. “***Veq, your opponent decided I’m a better target.. Finish with the bear and get over here.***” Not waiting for any sort of response, Lexi does the only thing that comes to her mind when she finds herself potentially flanked and outnumbered. Reaching into the Force, she grabs hold of Drax with the intention of hurling him towards the hovering Zoldo.

Drax had been drawn by his pathological hatred of the Jedi towards Lexi's scent in the Force. Charging, his saber-claws made ponderous swipes at Lexi's nimble form. One claw had landed, managing to score a glancing strike and leaving 3 gashes as Lexi just barely dodged out of the way of the attack, the attack drawing a mark but thankfully not penetrating deeper. He growls a frustrated animalistic electronically-modified growl.

content: ***"Acknowledged."*** Veq is wrestling with the Ursa, its slobbering and snapping jaws just centimeters away from Veq as he's holding the massive bear back, straining his new body against the massive bulk of the wild feroucious beast. It would seem the bear has the upper hand, as Veq's eyes glow yellow and lines of yellow ignite along the crevices of his nano-tubercular armor, momentarily empowering him and giving him enough strength to lift the bear above ground. The Ursa yelps in surprise, as Veq body slams it back down with incredible force of impact. To finish off the Dark Summons, Veq bends his wrist, as a single small thin Charybdis Spike shoots from his wrist into the back of the bear's cranium, coming out and protruding from its mouth, as the creature had squealed and then stopped all movement. The green magiks empowering it began to unravel, and it eventually burned up into a cold green flame, disintegrating completely.

Lexi reaches out in the Force and wraps her will around the bulky form of Inquisitor Drax. Sensing his resistance, her plan to throw the monstrosity at the hovering Zoldo changes. Instead she focuses and sends Drax careening into an industrial grade lumen post leaving the post bent around the Inquisitor’s form as he slides to the ground. The move also has the benefit of putting the Inquisitor back in quick range of the now freed up Veq to finish off the beefy Inquisitor.

Drax was seriously damaged by the Chosen One as the Force-imbued Lexi poured a ridiculous amount of Force Energy into empowering her Telekinetic Throw. Drax' mass, for once, worked against him this time around, as all of it came to a sudden and violent stop, punctuated by the overly loud sound of creaking metal as the Lumen bent under the impact. Slamming headlong into the solid industrial-grade oversized L-shaped durasteel Lumen Post, the Inquisitor could feel internal gears and ribs alike shattering at the impact, leaving a U-shaped depression in the lamp post. The buried base of the post had come undone, completely unraveling from the ground and buckling, allowing the post to tip over. Drax had regained his footing, his body vibrating and thrumming with unrestrained rage, somehow the Sith Rage had overpowered all of his ability to feel pain. The Inquisitor caught the Pillar in both hands and moved his hips to twist the hand-held weapon, wielding it as a massive Bat against Veq - who was charging into the fray to help Lexi as fast as possible, by her command. Lexi watched as, with a slow ponderous lazy creak of metal Drax had picked up and swung his newest armament. There was seemingly no way for Veq to dodge, as he was charging headlong into the path of the arcing improvised weapon.
