content: Zoldo had been awaiting his chance to catch the Jedi alone, enacting the plan he had been plotting to get back at the tricky grenade-chucking telekinetic mortar of Verdant energy and seemingly unparalleled martial skill. Summoning his darkest aspect, the vein at the corner of Zoldo's crow's feet pulsed and throbbed with more hatred than ever before. Enacting his Signature Spell, he materialized 3 Psychokinetic Clones - perfect duplicates of himself, who each moved independently. This took most of his energy for the round, but now he had quadrupled his effectiveness and increased his chances of being being missed by the same amount. Three of the Psy-Clones began hovering downwardly on their respective Red Disks, in preparation of an assault.

***Predictable~***Zern, while continuing to glide around the arena, had been intent upon finishing up another summoning spell, this time he had figured to try and overburden Lexi's team with too many enemies to fight. Waving his fingers in the air above the flapping evil tome, he cursed out the next incantation in Ancient Sith -- the spell brought fourth an army of evil little Gremlin Demons. Saving enough Force energy to inact a simple Green Force Shield for defense, he was ready as Yutra's predictible little Enmitic Oil napalm bomb glanced off. What was a lot less predictable was when Yutra saved the last 2 Enmitic Fireballs for bombarding his newest army of Dark Minions. After the scathing immolation, one lone Gremlin remains, looking around comically as its comrades are all immolated and running around on fire in their death throes.

Veq can't dodge in time as a wedge of steel suddenly and abruptly comes to encompass his whole vision, just the instant before he is impacted with an ungodly force by the swinging durasteel beam. A massive shockwave is emitted from the site of the impact where the unstoppable force had met the immovable object. Immovable until now that is, as Drax' revenge was finally realized - and returned to Veq in kind. The crab-like alien took the hit surprisingly well, his scientifically enhanced body able to absorb shocks to an incredible degree. What Veq enjoyed less so, was the impact of his spine against the tree he crash-landed against. The impact was so violent, that tree was killed from that day forth. A splintering of wood had folded inwardly, and Veq slumped down. Lexi hears from sickening crack in too vivid detail, as she can almost hear his internal organs cracking apart like prime lobster at a seafood eating contest. Veq's mouth drained purple blood, and he seemed to go limp.

Skarn had decided playtime with the sexy Reaper was over. He had enjoyed watching her breasts jiggle around as she bounded back and forth in one useless attempt after another to catch him out. Its true she scored many hits and was talented, but her efforts were ultimately useless, only serving to further fuel his Sado-Masochistic sexual urges as he became more engorged with the Dark Side than ever before. He couldn't wait to capture the little whore in chains and make her scream in pain as he played with her. Concentrating his malevolent Dark Side urges into enacting his own Transdimensional Summoning ability, he found the Pocket Dimension he used to store his Torture Equipment and called upon two massive loops of anchor chain. Opening both portals in the palms of his outstretched hands, two gigantic lines of chain shot forth, each tipped with a wicked and masochistically painful, albeit aerodynamic, grappling hook -- aimed squarely for Zyelle.

Zyelle was running forward back sideways and laterally every which direction she could think in an attempt to out-duel the sadistic vampire, but he was just too skilled. He must be insanely gifted, to have such martial skill. The Sith had all the while been mentally summoning his own attack of course, and as the two gigantic chains came flying out of his hands, Zyelle had to summon the Force into her legs, ankles and feet once more to enhance her agility, using both grappling hooks as stepping stones and then landing atop the unspooling chain, using it as a running lane to get closer to Skarn and scoring two solid blows to his strange, almost immortal body which once again only led to sounds of enjoyment and mocking laughter as two new yellow crags appeared across his torso in a cross-wise configuration. She got the sense he was enjoying this sick game.

Zern was enjoying reading the pages of his Dark Tome. After having read the Chapter on the Demonic Ursa aloud, he had proceeded to read the Page with the Gremlin Army. Turning to the next page of its own volition, the Sith Tome, which he knew was called the Demonomicon, presented him with another Dark Minion. Reading forth the evil Sith words, strangely his voiced echoed around the arena, where there really was no echo from anyone else. After a few seconds of harsh speech, Zern's voice peaks and a new critter is summoned forth from the Netherworld - a Demonic Locust, oversized dripping with acidic saliva and bristling with nasty insectoid hair follicles - each is a lethal blade sharpened to precision by years of evolution.

Yutra had been doggedly pursuing her quarry all the while, telekinetically floating through the air whilst conjuring more and more Enmitic Oil. Each time Zern had escaped death by her hands only brought about more hatred welling up from within Yutra. He had violated her once before, cast one of his little spells and absolutely had his way with her: that had humbled her and made her into a frightened little puppy anytime he was near her. Now, all of that pain and hatred had started to well up from within, she found a bottomless reserve of hatred was her's... had she only to reach for it. Nevertheless, somehow or other, her strange conversation with that Jedi, had irrevokably altered the course of her entire life. For once in her life, she had seen someone show her compassion. This seemed to steal much of the hatred Yutra had for Zern... but not all of it. Summoning two oversized Enmitic Fireballs, she skillfully directed one, then the other to crash into Zern. These were substantially more massive, causing them to break apart and cover Zern's Green Shield Bubble with a layer of Enmitic Napalm that turned his defensive Bubble into an autoclave oven.

Laughing meniacally at his newest creation, he once again threw a Force Bubble over himself as he once more saw the stubborn Sith witch throw her hateful missiles his way. This time, he was sure, she couldn't penetrate his defense. He was right... to a point. Even though the Napalm never touched him, its heat certainly did. Unbenownst to him, a small ember from an earlier Fireball that had landed in the pages of his tome was suddenly given the impetus to grow with the ever-increasing heat inside his Force Dome. The book spontaneously catches fire, immolating like a dry twig. Zern lets out a wicked howl as he sees the invaluable ancient book of Sith Secrets go up in smoke, powerless to do anything but gape in horror.

Spotting the trio of Zoldo clones heading her way, Lexi frowns as she discerns the likelihood of catching them all within a sweep of Verdant Force energy. Not waiting for them to get close enough to charge at her, she summons the plants around her to grow, surged on by the energy of the Verdant Force. Vines shoot out to grapple the trio of clones, drawing them in towards the maws of evolving carnivorous man eaters.

Zoldo watches in abject astonishment as 3 of his Clones get eaten up by the Park below, as Lexi summons her Verdant Push. After picking up his jaw off the floor, Zoldo smiles, knowing he has plenty more where those Clones came from, and he could sense that the Jedi used up part of her precious Force reserves to summon that little impromptu garden. ***Not to worry, she can't get me up here and I can get her.*** Summoning 4 new Psy-Clones, he decided not to make the same mistake this time around, and spread out the Force Projections more widely. He also decided not to send them down to attack just yet, waiting to see what other suprising maneuver the Jedi might attempt. Instead of attacking her, the Clones might be more useful in attacking her friends. ***Mheheheh... Yesssss...*** Once again, the Psychokinetic Projections were only now materializing into being, unable to act until getting their bearings, a small side effect of cloning his enormous intellect. However... it was only a matter of time, the Empire's victory was inevitable. The only way Lexi was going to be able to dispel the solid illusions would be to finish off Zoldo himself, or if he somehow managed to run out of Force reservers with which to summon more copies of himself. And given his enormous IQ, that wasn't likely to happen anytime soon. He had made one small mistake however, by putting himself in the center of the formation, he made it rather easy to tell which Zoldo was real, and which were streaming in the four cardinal directions away from him.

Each of the clones had a fraction of Zoldo's Force ability, as that was the only thing that could not be duplicated on a whim, the Psy-Clones were still far above the battlefield and spreading out to cover a wider area.
Lexi realizes she can potentially Force Pull Zoldo down to the ground, if she can just focus on the small dot of his shape in the distance and concentrate enough power to yank him away.

Zooming in to the action closer to ground level, Drax is attempting to bash Veq against the tree with another swing of the steel beam, rearing back and swinging the massive improvised weapon at the dazed, slowly rising nano-mutant Reaper.  Wiping purple blood from his mouth with the back of his wrist, Veq is able this time, to see the attack coming and nimbly hop-flip over the beam, clearing it like an Olympic gymnast. The tree gets rung instead, shaking leaves falling off.  Just as Veq thinks he's in the clear however, a new threat emerges. The buzzing Demonic Locust, dripping with acidic saliva and thrumming with evil green energy comes flying in at Veq. It seems to take offense at Veq's outward shape, thinking him to be a mating rival perhaps, and seeing Veq jump perfectly just now confirmed that bias. The Locust charges at Veq, attempting to fly by and slash him with a whole arsenal of sharp insectoid implements.

content: Veq hears the new threat approaching out of the corner of his ear-tentacle thanks to his enhanced hearing. Pretending not to see the Locust coming, at the very last second he dodges and grabs the overgrown grasshopper by its muscled leg, clasping it firm in his grasp, and then wrapping both arms around its body, squeezing and popping the crunchy thorax with his bare hands. The insect thrums angrily, but all of its implements are pointed downwardly, whereas Veq has grappled the creature from behind, where its delicate wings are now being folded and torn to mush. Veq's body pulses with yellow energy and he hugs the Dark Minion even tighter, the new constriction producing even more crunching noises as the grasshopper folds like a cheap piece of construction paper in on itself. Veq's chimeric DNA activates just then, unbidden, and senses the life leave the insect - absorbing much of its Essence into Veq. Veq's earlier wounds partially heal and he has now added to his spare DNA reserves. Veq decides to finish off this critter in a similar fashion to the last, shoving a body spike from within his carpal tunnel underneath its chin -- the spike protrudes from one multifaceted compound-eye and bursts it like an overripe fruit. The demonically infused insect ceases struggling and its green energies begin to unravel, unsummoning it from this dimension back to the netherworld.

Zyelle had closed the distance to her foe, running along the chains and jumping down to performing a whirling Triple Threat rotation to finish the vampiric Inquisitor once and for all. However, the maniac had been waiting for just such a move. Unbeknownst to Zyelle, the chains were even now whipping back around to ensnare the arrogantly beautiful Reaper, thanks to Skarn's skill with directing the partly-sentient evil objects mid flight. Sensing his hunger, they began to reach for the agile Reaper. Zyelle successfully avoided the first snare, but it caused her to lose her line of attack on the Inquisitor. Just then, the second chain snapped around Zyelle's waist and wrapped her tighter than a vice. Her ribs cracked and her breath left her lungs as the massive crushing links of chain wrapped over and over again mummifying her, constricted breasts partially squeezing out the top of the effective cylinder of metal that had encircled her. Skarn was smiling wickedly, knowing that finally, it was going to be his turn to have fun with her. All of the damage Skarn had incurred would be healed using his chains' abilities to regenerate his Cursed Body of wounds, when he was using them to torture his victim. This typically meant that Skarn would allow his victims to spend themselves tired attacking his body, and when the moment was just right, capture them for torture. However, this time... things didn't go exactly as planned.

Zyelle was caught fast in the Inquisitor's trap, so she decided to tap into her Force reserves and activate her trump card: time manipulation. All of a sudden, her scene stopped, and played backwards to the time Zyelle had been lining up her attack pattern. Zyelle experienced in reverse the pain of the massive chains un-crushing her rib cage, until she was free once more. Then, time resumed flowing forward. The second go around, events played out much differently. Anticipating all of his attacks, Zyelle ducked and dodged every chain perfectly, using brief moments of respite to complete her Triple Threat technique. At the very end, Skarn was left with 2 deep gashes, adding to those already present. The massive 'X' in the center of his chest burst outwardly with malignant yellow-orange light, as the Sith magiks holding him together began unraveling. That day, the Curse was forever lifted, as glowing evil orange light also emanated from each previous gash and slash Skarn had sustained in this fight. Bubbling up and dancing his body like a marionette, the Inquisitor's form exploded in a violent release of dark energy. Zyelle had already made her way clear thanks to her agility, and didn't bother to turn back to look at the explosion. She had to take a moment to clear the psychic pain of being crushed and uncrushed, but she knew she was walking away from that fight alive, unlike her opponent. Smirking, she turned to her next target, bidden by Lexi's telepathic call - the formidable monster cyborg, Drax.
**"Two down. Four to go."**
She was racking up quite the kill tally.

Inquisitor Zern can't hardly contain his animosity towards Yutra any longer. The Demonomicon was... it was gone, destroyed, burned to ashes. It was the greatest artifact in his possession, and was destined to help him rise to greatness within the Empire. Now, all of those dark dreams were shattered thanks to this sniveling little whelp. No matter, he would fix her, he just knew it. Spreading out his fingers as if in anticipation of playing some electronic instrument, he tightened his throat and incanted the familiar words he had used to enchant her the last go round. It was a nice evening, he recalled. That night he was particurly starved for companionship and the shapely figure of the youngest female Inquisitor was especially alluring to him. He had of course approached her in a gentlemanly way at first, but she had politely refused him. That night, at the last hour of them being together, he had imbibed so much alcohol that he was surprised he could even remember the words of this spell... Enchantment. Soon, he knew, she would be his plaything once again. Her eyes had become glassy and she floated down... as then all of a sudden her demeanor changed to one of rage and determination. She was only playing dead, waiting for an opportunity to launch her own trump card at him. Unbelievably, the firewitch had tricked him -- instead of becoming his willing slave in every manner imaginable, choosing to hurl her largest fireball as yet directly at Zern. Only instinct allowed him to bring up his Defense Globe barely just in time, but even still the fireball singed his sleeve and caused a small burn along his forearm.

Grand Inquisitor Yaga was most displeased as she witnessed and felt within the Force, Skarn's dying explosion. The Curse she had placed upon the handsome sculpted body of that most perverse ex-Inquisitor. It was finally broken by this... Walking Test Tube Experiment. **"Must I do everything myself!? You incompetent buffoons can't even take on three little girls and their pet crustacean. Despite all of your power, all of your muscles, all of your bravado... You are all useless inept incompetent fools. It seams I still have much teach you. Well, pay attention... Class is in Session. Lesson One. It takes a Woman to Kill a Woman."** Yaga takes a running start off the bridge and jumps into the air above the arena. A single encroaching line of green fire plays over her body briefly, top to bottom, transforming her clothes into that of scarlet silk... and revealing her true upraising, that of a Nightsister Sith Witch. Grand Inquisitor Yaga glides along as if on invisible winds, really just a simple spell known to all high level Sisterhood Initiates. Summoning a faint black hex-doll silhouette of Zyelle in front of her left hand, Yaga took her red lightsaber in her other hand and swiped it across the hex doll, eliciting spirit-damage to appear on the flesh of the young Reaper, where the slash had been on the corresponding part of the doll's body.

content: Zyelle turns to regard the newest threat as the Grand Inquisitor had finally fallen for her taunts at last. She had turned out to be a Nightsister, something Zyelle has heard of before. They are a wicked cult of sister Force users based out of Dathomir. Apart from that fact her hypno-education had imparted, it was noticeably absent in regards to exactly what abilities the Sisterhood had at their disposal on the battlefield. Zyelle's 20/5 vision picked up the vague shape of a Cruse Doll in her own likeness, materializing in the air floating before Yaga who was no doubt prearing to do something unsightly to Zyelle's body. Zyelle summoned her incredible, genetically perfect Force abilities - known colloquially as *Psionic Package Artemis Theta*. The Gene Insert immediately synchronized with her cerebellar genetically enhanced perfectly cultivated brain fibers, and enacted the first in a series of possible countermeasures against Sith Sorcery. In this case in particular, her Reaper DNA instructed Zyelle to blow away the strand in the ethereal realm of the Force that connected Zyelle's Soul to that of the Curse Doll, as that is what the Sith Witch had just done by enacting her own Spell. Summoning a concentrated focus of Force Power within her mind, and releasing it with the flat of her hand, she managed to easily blow away the critical connecting strand, dispelling the Curse Doll milliseconds before Yaga had sliced her lightsaber across the image of the doll... it's form dispersing into the wind, dispelled.
**"Thanks for joining the party, Granny. I think your arthritis must be acting up, 'cause your little party trick just failed utterly. Don't break that hip when you stick your landing, m'kay?"**

Drax catches Veq in a double-Nelson, coming from the rear and putting the crabman into what is inside wrestling considered an illegal move because it is impossible to break out of. Veq's shoulders behind to crunch in their sockets, as Drax uncomfortable breathy electronic breathing is bearing down his ear, punctuated by malicious laughter.

content: Yutra continues to stay at short range, her being this close to her abuser has triggered a massive amount of PSTD anger within her, her Rage bubbling over and coalescing into physical Enmitic Oil telekinetically shaping two massive Enmitic Fireballs, one slightly larger than the other. Both slam against Zern's Force Shield and the first one cracks it halfway open, while the other practically shatters is completely. Zern gets blasted with explosive detonating flaming Napalm, melting his skin as it runs down his body it ignites forming a hideous scar across his face, as if he were half melted by candlewax.
**"How do you like that, Zern. Feeling helpless now?"**
**"YEOOOOOAa-aAGHHH!!!~!"**

Zoldo had been surprised to feel a Force Snare catch around his ankle. The Jedi bitch was responsible, he instinctively realized. At the same time, he also knew and could feel that this attack was empowered by the Force, and that meant it could be countered by his own modicum of Force Power applied carefully and just in the right amount. Pouring his psionic energy into resisting her Force Snare, Zoldo managed to stop himself from slipping off his disk and plummeting approximately 200 meters straight down, into the nicely manicured and sharply upward-pointing tree branches. It took all of his enormous potential to do so, necessitating him to break concentration on the Four Psy-Clones, but he nevertheless managed to break Lexi's Force Snare and stop himself from falling off his Energy Disk. Unfortunately he had banged his head as he hit the Disk head on initially, and now he had a mild headache from the concussion. This bitch was getting to be more of a hassle than normal. ***No matter, with Inquisitor Yaga finally here... This shouldn't last much longer***

Zyelle hops and skips along the park rocks and lands on one of the unoccupied Red Energy disks recently summoned by the Psy-Clones, the Telekinetic Disk spell still continuing in its normal duration. Using its last few seconds on this planet, Zyelle rides the disk like a hoverboard and executes a picture perfect Triple Threat. Inquisitor Yaga is so mesmerized by the attack that she reacts a split second too late, Zyelle's blue blade severing Yaga's neck -- or nearly doing so, as the blue blade severs just her voice box, but stops short of cutting the windpipe. Blood wells up in Yaga's throat as she couldn't anticipate the perfectly executing whirlwind attack due to its lighting fast execution and mesmizing perfect form. Luckily, the cut had just stopped short of decapitating her right then and there.

Zoldo had been preoccupied watching the Fight of the Century from his perch atop the apex of cavern, when he had just seen the pink-haired girl rebound off one of his Clone's disks and nearly decapitate the Grand Inquisitor. Luck or simple Fate had interfered at the last second, the Disk duration expiring just short of allowing the Reaper to decapitate the old widow. With the Grand Inquisitor out of the picture... So many possibilities suddenly ran through Zoldo's head. He momentarily lost concentration on his Tug of Force Willpower game he was having with the persistent Jedi who had been attempting to pull him off his own elevated position. Unfortunately, that moment was all that Lexi needed to Pull Zoldo right off his Disk with the power of an unshakable Telekinetic Grip. Feeling himself pulled down to the Park below, he suddenly didn't think his future was going to be as bright as he dreamed.

It's only with her training provided by the Jedi Lexi is able to remained focused as Zyelle enacts her signature move. The feat however provides the distraction she needs as Zoldo's attention diverts to the action and she's able to yank the Inquisitor from his *very* high perch on his floating disk and send him careening dangerously towards the ground, propelled further by her Force imbued minipulations. He lands on the bridge with the sickening sound of broken bones. With the ending of his life, so does the Telepathic Link which sends psionic feedback  to his remaining allies. Zern's experiences a traumatic internal hemorrage in his breain, one eye rolls back into his head and he falls dead. His body dropping limply to the ground where he'd been Levitating.
Lexi doesn't have time to contemplate her next action as she turns to the last remaining obstacle she can reach and currently had a death grip on her ally, Drax.

Veq had been grappled into submission by Drax, who was slowly squeezing the life out of the mutant Reaper. The crabman's vision was growing dim, and he could tell that he would soon lose consciousness as Drax had crushed all of his neck vasculature with his kneecap and heavy durasteel greave on Veq's throat. Veq could almost hear his neck vertebrae snapping, or threatening to snap. Then all of sudden, everything changed. A psionic blast came seemingly out of nowhere and stunned Drax momentarily. Drax was so puzzled his body spasmed and loosened its grip of the full Nelson for just a brief instant. All of the Reaper's internal genetic programming, years centuries and eons of genetic evolution told him that this was his *last chance* to survive this fight. Elbowing Drax in the nads, Veq smoothly stood up from the chokehold and grabbed the monstrous robot-Sith by the back of his neck as gently as cradling a babe, twisting to trip the burly saberclaw expert, whist with his other hand whipping up his ankle up at an uncomfortable angle. This had the desired effect of tripping Drax into a downward fall, while Veq had positioned his knee to expertly land square in the back of his spine. A deafening, and altogether human - not robotic - crack of bone like splintering wood confirmed that somewhere deep inside, Drax did have a human spine. Unfortunately now, it had just been turned to a mass of quivering bleeding jelly by Veq's Signature Move - The Spinebreaker.
**"!~CRACKLE!~CHRUNCH~!"**

content: Zyelle's heart sinks as Yaga spurts blood from her neck wound, clearly unable to cast any of her Sith Sorceries. Looking at Yaga directly in the eyes, Zyelle realizes that Yaga also realizes that her major advantage of Spellcraft just evaporated with that perfect spinning attack landing upon her vocal cords.

Yaga still does have Mystic Flight enabled however, so she is able to glide upward and away out of Zyelle's reach. The Reaper was helpless to reach the Nightsister now. Just as Yaga was just thinking that she was safe and out of the reach of danger... two massive Empowered Enmitic Fireballs slam into her shoulder blades. The double-explosion rocks the entire sky above the park. Nothing is left of Yaga except a single thread of red silk, gracefully settling down across Zyelle's face. If that didn't get the Prison Security's Attention, Zyelle didn't know what would. Even though this was a designated sparring arena for Inquisitors to sharpen their skills against one another... This level of carnage was out of character even for the sparring rink.

content: Lexi finds herself standing amidst a ruined park, her party members looking at each other, stumbling survivors of carnage, with soot on their faces from the massive double-explosion.
**"Oops. I think I left something in the oven a bit too long..."**
Yutra shrugs languidly, as if half apologetic, half bragging.
**"Jedi. Thank you for saving my life."**
Zyelle puts her hands on her hips haughily: **"I still got 2 of em. Let's see... 2 for our glorious leader. And... 1 apiece for Veq and Yutes. You have some catching up to do it seems."**
**"Really though, I would have had 4 kills if you hadn't stolen like 2 of em... Oh well, I did get to fight in the opening match, which no one else here did, so I do have to share after all."**
**"Dark Lady. The Force guided you into our fold. I too once served the Empire. Now I am free, and I willingly pledge my service to the Jedi. The Chosen One."**
**"You must be dumber than you look if you believe that."**
**"I am aware my current appearance is bizarre... A moment please."**

Veq focuses and all around sense him tap into his Force skills. His massive mutant spikes retract gradually, sliding back and coalescing back into the normal shape of the Reaper, although not exactly the same, as each time this transformation occurs his nano-trabecular armor becomes altered in random evolutionary martial genetically optimal ways. At least Veq was back down to Medium size, even his mass had somehow retracted back and away, which was the most bizzare thing of all. His voice had improved as well, no longer monstrous, but rather unnaturally calm, but at the same time filled with emotion. It still had a monstrous underbass tone as he spoke, but it only added to his gravitas instead of scaring away small children like usual.

Lexi breathes heavily, her progress towards Veq to aid him stopped short by the explosion which incinerates the Inquisitor's leader. The battleground bears its own scars now from the carnage as the 5 Inquisitors they had faced against were now no more. Unlike her companions, she didn't take pride in their kills, only in the fact they had suceeded. She doesn't join in on their jubilations as she turns in a slow circle, her expression inscrutible as she adds the Inquisitors to her mental tally of those she'd had a hand in killing. 
Finally, Lexi joins the others as Veq's transformation completes. She's been surprised when he'd appeared transformed, but to be able to revert back to some measure of his previous self was astonishing. Her expression reflects as much. "**Is this a byproduct of the virus. Were you able to figure out how to control the transformation?**"
**"Virus? What virus?"**
**"Fear not, the Virus is long gone. What it has left in its wake however, is my Twi'lek body now has novel transformative abilities."**
**"Hrmph. You're not the first Twi'lek male to claim to be virus free... but you're certainly.... *different*."** Her gaze seems unreadable.

"**It's a bit of a long story, but it does have to do with the Super Reaper currently holed up on the Water Treatment level, which I would recommend not drinking any water that's not been bottled prior to today.**"
Yutra seems to narrow her eyes, looking back and forth between Lexi's face and Veq's. **"Yeeahhhhh... No kidding huh."**
**"Hey Firecracker, I think we'd better beat it before more of your um, coworkers, arrive."** She thumbs in the direction of Serenity.
**"I recommend to collect the enemy's resources and then retreat, wether Bishop was rescued or not... I think you should Comm Magnet and see what the status of the rescue mission is."**
"**He was jamming the comms,**" Lexi reminds Veq. "**We'll have to meet up with him, or just go to the infirmary ourselves.**"
"**The poison will be wearing off by now, so if he hasn't been rescued then Bishop is going to be in a trouble**"
**"Yes, we will have to wait for him to realize our fight is over, or wait until he has completed his mission, before we can Comm him."**
**"Traveling now to the infirmary is not advised, since none of us are stealth specialists... We have to trust our friends to pull through."**
**"Unless.... We have an infiltrator."** He looks over at Yutra and pauses.
Lexi nods in agreement. "**Then let's collect what we need from Yutra's old 'co-workers' and rendeavous with them in Serenity.**"
**"What are you looking at bug boy."**
She stops and looks at Yutra, but for a different reason. "**Unless you've decided to go your own way?**"

**"Our colleague Magnet has been jamming all communications just now prior to the start of this altercation."** He gestures at the wrecked park benches and smoking fireball craters. **"All this was a Giant Diversion, so that our troops could whisk a captured team member named Bishop. He was the one undergoing a 'simulated stroke', whom Drax nearly finished off."**
**"You want me to go check to see if your friend escaped, and what if he hasn't?"**
**"Save him."**
Yutra looks over at the Jedi next, pondering something.
**"Let me guess, under auspices of taking him back to Interrogation?"**
**"Precisely. You have a mind for this, it would seem. *Dark Lady.*"**
"**You would need to convince the Inquisitor escorting him he's no longer needed as well in order to bring him back to us in Serenity.**"
**"Serenity?"**
"**The garden. It's what Zyelle calls it.**"
**"Its a better name than Nature Reserve X-11-Kappa"**
**"Hmmm. Serenity... I like that."**
**"Its got a nice clean waterfall... Good place to wash away those pretty tears."**

Nods. **"Fine. I'll do it. Trust me, I'll be back. And I'll meet you by the Waterfall. I know the one you mean."** Without another word, she turns and levitates immediately in the direction of the Infirmary.
**"Is it wise to put the entire mission in the hands of an Inquisitor?"**
**"Well you're a Reaper and so am I technically... So..."**

Lexi watches after Yutra for a moment, biting her lower lip as she considers. It wasn't too long ago the woman had tried to kill her, though she wasn't the first she had befriended in such a way. "**I just hope the squad doesn't attack her if she walks in on them.**" She turns back to the others. "**Split up, and gather anything useful.**" Remembering half the squad was waiting in the ventalition shaft she heads that way, hoping at least NZ was still waiting for them.

Without going too much into a prolonged scene. Basically, when Yutra arrives at the Infirmary she is immediately allowed to inspect the prisoner at her request. As Yutra walks in, she senses the squad as they were in the middle of rescuing Bishop. Yutra notices that they are also about to be caught by the Inquisitor who had been sent to escort the prisoner here. Yutra makes use of her charms to delay the enemy Inquisitor, buying time for Bishop and friends to escape back into the vents. As soon as the ventilation grate closes, the enemy Inquisitor enters the 'Treatment Room' alone to find it empty. He curses under his breath and turns back to look in Yutra's direction. The scene cuts out abruptly.  Not far away... The squad is walking out of the Ventilation Shed, Bishop slung over Jumbo's shoulder as he gets patted by everyone and all the squad members are talking in excited voices. Lexi, Veq, and Zyelle had gathered as much resources as they could reliably carry away and had made their way to Serenity. They didn't have to wait long for the return and reunification of their whole group to be realized at last.

As the squad files out of the ventilation shed and joins her small company of Reaper companions by the waterfall, relief spreads through her. They’d done it, and survived without any losses. 
She pats each of the men in the squad on the arm, her grin spread across her face as she congratulates each of them. “**Well done,**” she says. As Jumbo lowers Bishop to the ground, she approaches to look him over. It was possible she hadn’t calculated the dose correctly, or the mushrooms may have some unintended side effects. 

“**There was a female Inquisitor helping us. Did you see her as you were rescuing Bishop?**” Lexi asks, wondering if Yutra would be joining them soon or if some ill had befallen her during the rescue.

**"She was hard to miss, Commander. Beautiful and graceful, pale perfect skin. If not for her, we'd have been caught and tortured by Inquisitors by now. We could see her talking in the next room over, trying everything in her power to give us the time to escape into the vents. It was Vulture's fault you know, he took a wrong turn in one of the air ducts so we had to double back to find him. Anyway, if not for this woman of exceptional beauty, we probably wouldn't be here right now."**
**"Hey, I was just following the direction I was given, whoever gave them was the true cause of this."**
**"Can it you two, doesn't matter now. We're all safe and mission was successful."**

Hovering straight down by unseen winds, Yutra lands gently 5 meters back from the main group. She brushes back her hair and gives everyone a smirk, putting one hit arrogantly on one hip. **"I see you all made it. You're welcome, by the way."**
**"That's her Commander!"**

Lexi had been listening to the exchange as she looks over Bishop but glances up as Yutra makes her appearance. "**Yutra! Yes, thank you. It seems my men were quite in the predicament.**" Satisfied Bishop was going to recover, she stands at last to greet Yutra properly. "**We got off on the wrong foot before, and I'm glad you've decided to stay with us.**" Giving the ex-Inquisitor a small bow, she adds, "**I'm Lexi Ardena.**"

Yutra puffs out her lower lip, narrowing her eyes at Lexi. Then, she relaxes and tosses her hair back once more, shaking it like a mane. **"I know. Like I said though, I'm on my own side. Your merry band owes me now, so I might as well make use of that debt. You will take me with you, wherever it is you're going after this."**
Getting huffy again, she looks at the group: **"Well?"** She crosses her arms, **"Aren't you gonna introduce me? You may call me... Inquisitor Yutra... or just Yutra."**
Lexi gives Yutra a small smile, "**Of course.** She leads Yutra through introducing each of the White Doves, Veq, NZ and Zyelle.

Yutra doesn't seem to notice any of the faces, not changing her expression except when it comes time to be formally introduced to Veq. **"I saw this one fight... Rather remarkable. I'd never seen anything like it before."** She walks up to Veq rather boldly, making a 360 walk around him and poking around his armor and private places. **"Impressive... What did you say you were called?"**
**"I am called Veq'neviz, Dark Lady. I am at your service."**
**"You said you were a Twi'lek. Did you have a mate?"**
**"No, Lady. I was a Reaper once, and never had a family to call my own."**
**"Very good... You will be my bodyguard and serve me from this day, understand? In return..."** She slips off one of her bra straps. **"You will have access to me any time you wish. Do you agree?"**

While the two of them talk she approaches Ghost and NZ offering them each one of the Red Keycards. "**We got these of the Inquisitors we fought. They may be able to provide access to the other areas.**"
**"!"** Veq stammers. **"I-I'm sorry, Dark Lady. I am sworn to serve another."**
Eyes glowing coal fire red, she returns her bra strap to its previous position. **"You shall pay for that, insolent insect."** She walks away to brood by the Waterfall alone.
**"Huh, haven't seen this type of Keycard yet."**
**"Splendid, Master. I shall add this to my databases."**
**"Thanks for coming back to get me, Commander. I was sure I was done for. When you're ready to speak, I need to catch you up on developments inside the prison."**

"**We'll need to see about retrieving Bishop's gear or alternatives. Could you to start brainstorming? Yutra may be able to help, but she doesn't seem in the mood to talk further.**" Lexi watches Yutra stalk off, raising a questioning eyebrow to Veq before going over to join Bishop.
"**Any lasting effects from the poison?**" she asks him, taking a seat next to him. Knowing all was well she asks her next question. "**What did you learn?**"

**The shrooms? Naw, I used to do way harder stuff back in my Stormtrooper Academy days."** Sitting down on the beach, he throw a rock into the water. **"The Nighthawks are in Block C. I'm fairly sure Wraith is in Block A, that's an educated guess. There are lots of dissidents imprisoned all around, that could help us overrun this base if freed."**

**"Now that most of the Inquisitors overseeing Block B are all out of commission, that prison block is vulnerable to a riot. There's only 2 Inquisitors left for the whole entirety of that Block, from what I'm led to understand."**
**"The rest are just normal prison guard troops, not any kind of Force Sensitives."**

"**Starting a riot in Block B could give us the cover needed to rescue the Nighthawks and Wraith. In the process, we could free as many dissidents that would aid us in causing enough of a distraction to get off this level.**"
"**Block A from what Zyelle has mentioned is where they keep Force users and other high profile prisoners. They'll probably have a high number of Reapers and Inquisitors guarding the block.**"
**"Be careful Commander. Each block is overseen by a separate Grand Inquisitor. I heard your goth friend already killed one, so that leaves 3 more out there."**

Lexi frowns as she considers his warning.  It had taken them nearly everything they'd had to defeat the last group of Inquisitors.  If they had to contend with the same number in two more blocks they would be in trouble. "**Perhaps we can play the same ploy we did and have them sent to the Infirmary.**"
**"That will only work a limited number of times... The instant one of those Inquisitors turns to run and warn the others, the jig's up."**
**"I think... if you're discovered, that's when you should pull the trigger on the Block B riot. That will divert all the Inquisitorial attention away from you onto the rioters instead."**

"**The challenge with that becomes coordination.  We'd need someone on the inside ready to spring the riot. We also need to locate Wraith and the Nighthawks before then to make the best use of the opportunity.**" She pauses. "**How did you come by this information? NZ looked through prisoner records and Wraith wasn't listed as having been captured.**"
**"Lunch hour rumors. Could be wrong for all I know."**
**"Nighthawks were captured so it stands to reason so was Wraith, but I can't say for sure."**
**"Ultimately we have to just take over the base HQ and that'll solve everything, but we can't unless we have a strong enough collection of men to take it by force."**

**"In regards to someone on the inside, I met plenty of men there hoping against hope to leave that forsaken place. Nobody wants to be tortured by those sadists, those so-called Inquisitors. They will cut off their right hand to get out I can guarantee that."**

Lexi nods.  "**It's a start at least. Yutra may be able to fill in the blanks to formulate  a better plan. Though our plans rarely go as planned,**" she gives him a small smile. "**We'll need to find your kit or get you a replacement. Know where they are storing it?**"
**"No idea."**
"**We'll figure out something,**" Lexi says with a shrug. "**Whats the layout like for Block B? Group cells?**"
**"Individual cells mostly."**
"**Which means finding a way to unlock cells or time when a group of the prisoners in the block are together.**"
**"Maybe there's a control room that unlocks the cells?"**
"**Yutra will likely know. For now though,  we need to rest and recover from the ordeal with the Inquisitors. Then we'll plan the next moves to rescue the Nighthawks.**"
"**Is there anything else you want to report?**" she asks, preparing to take her leave from Bishop and head over towards the waterfall.
**"Nothing. Thanks for saving my life, Commander."**
At the Waterfall, Zyelle and Yutra are bathing, while the rest of the troops are ashore. They're all facing away from the waterfall and giggling..
**"We're keeping watch, heh."**
**"Let us know if you wanna clean up Commander we'll stay on guard for ya."**

Walking towards the shore Lexi looks at the scene in bemusement. The men were getting their own enjoyment out of the idea of the attractive women behind them bathing nonchalantly in the waterfall behind them. Unfortunately,  the women were also extremely dangerous and mercurial.  "**Just remember to keep watch away from the water. Especially if you ever have plans to look at another woman let alone enjoy their company.**" She smiles innocently with the warning as she passes the line of men on the way to the water. She left it open as to whether it was a threat from her or a reminder of the dangers their newest allies posed.
Reaching the shore, Lexi follows the lead of the other women and stripping down. Her thoughts begin to wander as her soft, worn leather boots are soon joined by her slightly charred white Jedi robe and belt. It had been a long day and the thought of bathing was highly appealing. Before disrobing completely,  she bends down next to the water and extends her senses into the pool once more, using her gift with the Verdant Force to ensure it hadn't yet been corrupted.

**"Hello beautiful. I had hoped to see you strip naked, glad you aren't squeamish. Some Jedi can be a little up-tight. Inquisitors too."** She nods at Yutra who had her arms crossed and protecting herself from any peeking troopers.
***Not everyone is given perfect genetics from birth. You're incorrigible."**
**"Perfection is a subjective judgement, but thank you for the compliment."**

**"I can't believe I let you talk me into this..."** She stands up and finally uncrosses her arms, revealing her full languid form. Vulture develops a nosebleed spontaneously. A general chorus of wolf whistles wafts in from ashore. Yutra blushes a deep pink and lowers herself below water level.

Confident now the water was safe from the Corruption, Lexi rises in preparation of removing the rest of her clothes when the men behind her proceed to react to the happenings going on at the waterfall. It causes her to pause. The last thing she needed was for their tongues to wag when they got back to the Chimera. There were enough rumors and speculation circulating.
Zyelle's form had been concealed by the falling water, briefly stepping out the men could make out the shapely Reaper's bust and a few bite into their fists.
Her warning apparently had done nothing to override their debauchery.

Lexi turns and stalks over to them. "**Keep watch on the other side of the trees,**" she says, indicating a thick copse that would conceal the waterfall.  "**I better not catch you spying on us,**" she warns, her tone severe.
**"Yes Commander. Enough gawking, let's move it men."**
Lexi has stripped down to her pants and tank top exposing the thin lines of scars on her arms from her numerous lightsaber combats and the nasty scar above her heart where Vaz blade had pierced her.
**"Help yourself. Body wash and shampoo is on that little mossy rock."**

After waiting for them to finally leave and reaching out in the Force to make sure they had stationed themselves where she'd instructed, she returns to her stack of clothes and discards her remaining before joining the other two women. "**That's better,**" she remarks. Taking the indicated toiletries she starts with her hair.
**"Greetings Jedi Ardena... It's been so long since I relaxed, this feels so refreshing..."** She sighs languidly.
**"I'm jealous of you... Veq seems to like you over me ."**
Lexi pauses at her comment.  "**I don't know if it's necessarily that. He gave me a vow and he's intent on honoring it.**"
**"Veq!? Ha! You could have any of those men, or all at once, and you only have eyes for the virus victim? Can't blame you for having intent for a Reaper though."**
**"See? He's honorable. Something direly lacking in men I usually surround myself with ."**

Zyelle smiles conspiratorially. She goes under the water. **"Why limit yourself to males?"** Reemerging next to Yutra she pushes her breasts into her back, eliciting another blush from the Ex- Inquisitor. **"Don't be so dogmatic, Inquisitor. There are many flavors of pleasure to be enjoyed."**

**"Hey!"** She pushes Zyelle into the water too roughly, causing a loud splash. Zyelle seems to giggle at the game and begins splashing water back. Soon there is a small water fight and both are laughing.

Lexi laughs at the play as she stands under the fall to rinse her hair. As the water fight dies down she edges in closer to the pair. She directs her word towards Yutra. "**It's my experience,  Reapers have very little filters when it comes to the matter of sexuality. They're on constant overdrive and nearly insatiable.**" Lexi gives a bit of a shrug.
**"I've never dated a Reaper before... I didn't even think it was allowed."**
**"It's dangerous if you take a male Reaper to bed... Not so for a Reaper female though."** She winks playfully.
**"Dangerous?"**

Lexi looks over at Zyelle, "**Do Reapers even 'date'?**" she asks with some amused curiosity. "**I got the impression trysts are the preference.**" Lexi frowns as she regards Yutra and her question.  "**The males are more hardwired to procreate, but the baby usually kills the mother.**"
**"You seem to know a great deal about Reaper males..."** She looks over at Veq who was meditating with eyes closed atop the waterfall.
**"Just who are you people anyway? Where did you come from and why are you here?"**
**"Trysts? Depends on the Reaper but usually only a Reaper can date another for any prolonged period of time."**

"**When one is infiltrating the nest of the Reapers it's best to know as much as possible,**" she says with a wry smile. Noticing the look up at Veq, she adds,  "**I only met Veq today. I saved him from the virus which had begun to transform him after which he pledged himself to aid my cause. **"
**"He seems very dedicated to you... "**
**"I don't think it's a romantic bond if that's what you mean... Our fearless leader is already spoken for, aren't you."**

Lexi frowns as she considers her answer as to who they were and why they had come and thought is waylaid by Zyelle's probing question.  A slight blush forms on her skin. "**Why ever would you think that?**"
**"Let's just say it... Doesn't take a mind reader..."**
Yutra perks up. **"Oh? Care to share?"**
**"I met a very charming Reaper male who tried to seduce me and nearly succeeded too ... I felt he was bonded though, only later when I encountered Lex did I finally put two and two together."**
Lexi mutters, "**What is it with the galaxy and wanting to gossip about my love life.**"
**"Hey you're a lucky girl, if he wasn't spoken for I'd gladly have bedded him myself."**
**"Incorrigible..."**
"**It would have been his choice,**" Lexi says without jealousy.  "**We're not exclusive and I'm well aware I can't satiate him completely.**"
**"Oh really? What about all three of us then?"**
Blushes again. **"Hey! I don't even know who you're talking of..."**
At the suggestion Lexi does turn crimson. "**If we should survive this... let's discuss then alright?**"
She turns around away from the pair to grab at the soap and starts to scrub viciously at her skin.
Grinning at Lexi she adds, **"Can't scrub away the scent of a Reaper Jedi girl..."**

Lexi pauses for only a moment to look over her shoulder at the Reaper. "**Oh, I know. It just makes me feel better.**" she says with some bitterness. The scent she knew wasn't just physical, but part of their psyche as well.
**"Want me to help take him off your hands? Few males can resist my attention."**
**"Stop... How can you even ask that ."**
**"She knows they can't be together... He's just a diversion to pass the time anyway."**
**"Hey you... I won't let you talk to her in that way."** Pouts.
Grinning, Zyelle starts tickling Yutra under the armpits and enjoying her soft moans and squirming. **"Don't worry I haven't forgotten about you."**

"**She's right though,**" Lexi says turning back around. "**I'm a Jedi. We don't take mates even if being his wouldn't ultimately kill me. We're both ultimately on different paths. For right now, we walk it together, but one day that will change.**"
**"What path... You still haven't told me anything. Not that I can blame you, given my pedigree..."**
**"We ask the questions round here, Inquisitor. Now... Confess!"** Tickles her torso ravenously.
Lexi smiles at the antics, and is relieved at the diversion from having to answer Yutra regarding their mission. Her mission.

When there's a break from the tickling Lexi decides to start with the short term. "**Bishop learned that more of our men are being held in Block C. He is also under the impression Wraith was captured and is in Block A, but we have nothing so far to corroborate he was captured.**"
"**Our next step is to find out if our men are indeed detained and then rescue them.**"
**"I assume you'll want my help with that..."**

content: **"What's your plan?"**

Lexi sighs. "**I don't have one yet.  There's not enough information to make decisions. First we need to confirm if Wraith is in Block A. If he is, that spreads us out significantly.**" Lexi pauses as she thinks. "**We do however plan to use the reduced security in Block B and start a riot. Either to cover the jailbreak itself or our escape. Which means we'll need someone on the inside who can open the cells.**"
**"I can open those cells in Block B if you need me to, but the other Blocks are out of my jurisdiction unfortunately."**
"**Are you able to access prisoner records for the other blocks?**"
**"Yes, but so are you now thanks to those Red Keycards."**
"**Yes, but if I step out and an Inquisitor sees me? I can fool the guards and the Reapers but not your ex comrades.**"
It's Yutra's turn to sigh. **"I hope you don't expect me to do *all* the heavy lifting around here, but... I suppose it can't be helped, at least while we're here on Detention Level."**
**"Oh, I'd say you've been doing the *heavy lifting* alright, based off the size of that bust you're carrying around..."** She turns around, **"Hm what did I say something?"**

content: "**I believe a guard said there was a terminal in an administrative building.  We can use the vents to get there unnoticed. If it's occupied then getting there does little good.**" she gives a small shrug. "** Now with the keycards, my scouts will have an easier time gathering the information,  so let's see what they turn up before we expose you too much.**"
**"That's the spirit. Get the men to do all the work while we get some well deserved R&R."**
**"Exposing me too much? Too late..."**
**"Can't complain... I'm enjoying the view."**
